item.title: 使用 2 台显示器进行学习和工作 | Use 2 monitors for learning and work
item.description:
在封面图片上，我在第一台显示器上观看 youtube 教程，同时在第二台显示器上编写代码。

自从 3 年前我买了一个额外的显示器插入我的笔记本电脑，我无法想象没有它我自己会做任何严肃的事情。

作为一名 JavaScript 全栈开发人员，我总是学习新事物（前端、后端、移动、桌面、Chrome 扩展、报废、云部署等）

无论是有偿工作还是副业，工作流程大体相同。 为了感觉舒服，我需要同时在全屏 2 个应用程序中保持打开状态。

用例

  1. 我观看视频教程以了解有关新主题的基本想法，并在导师之后重复所有步骤

    • 第一台显示器 – youtube 视频
    • 第二个监视器 - 代码编辑器

  2. 我阅读文档/书籍并在思维导图中做笔记，以深入理解材料。

    • 第一台显示器 – 文档站点
    • 第二台显示器——思维导图编辑器

  3. 我编码

    • 第一个监视器 - 编码结果（网页或移动应用程序模拟器）
    • 第二个监视器 - 代码编辑器

当您只有一台显示器时，您应该：

  • 在全屏窗口/应用程序之间切换或
  • 应用程序之间的分屏

在这两种情况下，闪烁的全屏应用程序或管理部分屏幕打开的应用程序的宽度不足会占用您大量的认知能量，并且您会更快地感到疲倦。 此外，您可能会伤害您的眼睛。

所以两个显示器是解决方案。 同时打开的两个全屏应用程序让您在管理信息、吸收新知识和轻松完成工作方面拥有巨大优势。

例如，这就是我使用 2 个监视器对 NextJS 文档进行思维导图的方式：

硬件设置

如果你在谷歌上搜索“多显示器”之类的东西——然后查看图像，你就会对它们的设置产生错误的认识，因为很多图像都是关于水平设置的，这会在一天之内扼杀你的脖子。 相反，您应该选择垂直设置——这样您就可以将眼睛从一台显示器切换到另一台显示器，而不会拉伤脖子。

你不需要一个棘手的小工具架——任何大小合适的罐子或盒子都是合适的。 我多年来一直使用宜家的 Pyssla 罐子作为显示器的支架，它非常好：

操作系统设置

就像谷歌搜索一样，操作系统会将您推向水平设置。 但是，您可以更改桌面设置中的位置。 当操作系统设置跟随现实生活中显示器的位置时很好，这样您就可以直观地工作。

没钱买吗？

即使你是穷人：学生、转行、初入职场、缺钱，我强烈建议你尽快购买便宜的二手显示器，因为它有时会提高你的效率。

也许你已经注意到我的第一张照片：我的显示器很旧，有两条彩色垂直线的缺陷。 我的笔记本电脑很旧，没有钥匙“；/：”。 所以我什至羞于向你展示它们。 但它们一起是学习和工作的酷工具。 我更喜欢这样的一对，而不是我拥有的 mackbook。



原文:

On the cover image I'm watching youtube tutorial on 1st monitor and simultaneously write code in 2nd monitor.

Since I bought an extra monitor to plug into my laptop 3 years ago, I can’t imagine myself doing anything serious without it.

As a JavaScript full-stack developer I always learn new things (Frontend, Backend, Mobile, Desktop, Chrome extensions, Scrapping, Cloud deployment etc.)

Whether it’s a paid job or a side project, the working process is generally the same. And to feel comfortable I need to keep opened in full screen 2 apps at the same time.

Use cases

  1. I watch a video tutorial to get basic ideas about the new subject, and repeat all steps after the tutor

    • 1st monitor – youtube video
    • 2nd monitor – code editor

  2. I read documentation/book and take notes in mindmap for deep understanding of material.

    • 1st monitor – documentation site
    • 2nd monitor – mindmap editor

  3. I code

    • 1st monitor – result of coding (web page or mobile app emulator)
    • 2nd monitor – code editor

When you have only 1 monitor, you should either:

  • switch between full-screen windows/apps or
  • split screen between apps

In both cases, blinking full screen apps or managing lack of width in part screen opened apps will take a lot of your cognitive energy, and you get tired much faster. Also, you can harm your eyes.

So two monitors is the solution. Simultaneously opened, two full-screen apps give you a big advantage to manage information, absorb new knowledge and do your work with fewer efforts.

Example, that's how I mindmaped NextJS docs using 2 monitors:

Hardware setup

If you google something like “multiple monitors” – and look at images, you get the wrong idea about their setup, because lots of images are about Horizontal setup, which will kill your neck in one day. Instead, you should choose a vertical setup – so you can switch your eyes from one monitor to another without straining your neck.

You don’t need for that a tricky gadget holder – any jar or box with proper size is suitable. I use an Ikea's Pyssla jar as a stand for monitor for years, and it is pretty good:

Operational System setup

Just as google search, OS will push you to horizontal setup. However, you can change positioning in desktop settings. That relative position of 1 to 2 affects on how mouse cursor will be moving between monitors. It is good when OS settings follow along with real life monitors’ position, so you will work intuitively.

Have not money for it?

Even if you are a poor: student, career switcher, beginner in job, with lack of money, I highly recommend you to buy even a cheap second hand monitor as soon as possible, because it will increase your efficiency at times.

Maybe you have noticed on the first picture with me: my monitor is old, with defect of two colored vertical lines. And my laptop is old without a key ";/:". So I even ashamed to show them to you. But together they are a cool tool for study and work. I prefer such couple over mackbook that I have.
item.author: apayrus

item.title: 使用一台显示器作为程序员的完整指南。 | The Complete Guide To Using One Monitor As A Programmer.
item.description:
关于一台显示器是否优于多台显示器的争论已经持续多年。 在决定哪种显示器设置最适合您时，需要考虑许多因素，例如您的工作环境、显示器的尺寸和类型，以及您每天使用的应用程序数量。

无论您的需求是什么，总有一款适合您的显示器设置。 显示器有不同的尺寸、形状和分辨率。 最常见的是 30 英寸对角线尺寸。 这是大多数人的标准，并提供标准的观看体验。 多台显示器允许您同时在两个屏幕上展开工作，许多人认为这可以提供更好的整体工作环境，因为它可以帮助您专注于您正在做的事情。 但是，这是个人喜好问题，通常取决于您正在执行的任务。

在本文中，我们将讨论在日常工作中使用一台显示器的一些主要好处，以及有效利用显示器的键盘快捷键。 如果您是 Linux 用户，那么这篇文章绝对适合您，尤其是如果您使用的是 Ubuntu。 如果不是这种情况，请尝试寻找一些替代方案。

使用一台显示器的好处

一个常见的误解是一台显示器不足以进行编码。 实际上，它可能非常有效。
虽然，很多人多年来一直在使用多台显示器。 但是，如果您是真正编码的开发人员，那么仅使用一台显示器进行编码和开发会有一些很大的好处。

第一个好处是您可以节省桌面空间。 使用一台显示器，您不必担心每次需要编码或处理其他事情时清理办公桌。 这将使您的办公桌远离杂乱和干扰，以便您更轻松地专注于工作。

第二个好处是，如果屏幕分辨率足够高，您将更容易看到眼前的代码，而无需在两个屏幕之间来回滚动。

第三个好处是你的大脑将能够专注于手头的任务，因为屏幕不会分成两部分。 这意味着您的工作将更易于跟踪，并且您在工作时会更加清晰。我将拥有一台大显示器，但我可能会使用第二台显示器来做其他事情（例如浏览器） ）。

第四个好处是您将能够更好地执行多项任务，因为屏幕的“占地面积”将更小。 这意味着您的显示器占用的空间会更少，因此您不会在一个屏幕上看到两个或三个窗口。 这样可以更轻松地运行多个应用程序，而无需不断关闭它们。 例如，您可以打开一个电子表格和一个浏览器，并且可以在它们之间快速移动。

最后一个好处是屏幕更容易阅读，因为它的边框更小。 这意味着当您阅读屏幕上的内容时，您的眼睛不需要在屏幕上移动那么远，这将使他们感觉更舒适。

使用一台显示器的隐藏功能

使用一台显示器而不是多台显示器的主要优点之一是它迫使您一次专注于一项任务。

研究表明，多任务处理实际上会降低生产力和效率[0]。 它还可能导致更多的压力甚至身体疼痛[1]。 使用单个监视器可以降低发生这种情况的风险。 这是主要好处之一。 尝试同时查看两个屏幕时，它还可以减轻眼睛的压力，减少眼睛疲劳和头痛。

使用一台显示器的好处很多。 一台显示器更便宜、更方便，并且对于多任务处理更有用。 此外，在单个屏幕上与他人分享您正在做的事情比在多个屏幕上更容易。 在同一屏幕上比较两个对象也更容易。

多台显示器的主要缺点之一是它们通常比单台显示器更难移动和设置，即使经过专家调整也是如此。 另一个缺点是每个屏幕都必须由专家调整到正确的位置和尺寸，这比调整单个显示器更困难。

在下一节中，我将分享一些我每天使用的快捷方式列表，这些快捷方式可以帮助我在应用程序等之间轻松导航。

包起来

这构成了我们在日常工作中使用单个显示器的指南。 我们已经讨论了使用单个显示器的优点以及使用多个显示器的缺点。 我们还介绍了可帮助您轻松管理环境的键盘快捷键列表。

与往常一样，本文是送给您的礼物，您可以与任何您喜欢的人分享或以任何有利于您的个人和职业发展的方式使用它。 通过支持这个博客，你让我有动力发布与 python 相关的高质量内容。 预先感谢您的最终支持！

编码快乐，伙计们； 下一篇见。

参考

多任务的多重成本。 大脑。 2019;2019:cer-04-19. 2019;2019:cer-04-19。

[2] Ubuntu, Windows and workspaces . [2] Ubuntu、 Windows 和工作区。



原文:

The debate over whether one monitor is better than multiple monitors has been going on for years. There are many factors to consider when deciding which monitor setup is best for you, such as your work environment, the size and type of your display, and the number of applications you use on a daily basis.

No matter what your needs are, there is a monitor setup that will be perfect for you. Monitors come in different sizes, shapes, and resolutions. The most common one is the 30-inch diagonal size. It’s the standard for most people and provides a standard viewing experience. Multiple monitors allow you to spread out your work on two screens simultaneously, which many argue can provide a better working environment overall because it can help you to focus on what you’re doing. However, this is a matter of personal preference and often depends on the tasks you are performing.

In this article, we are going to discuss some of the key benefits of using one monitor on your daily job, along with keyboard shortcuts to efficiently utilize your monitor. If you are a Linux user, then this article is definitely for you, especially if you are using Ubuntu. If it is not the case, then try to find some alternatives.

👉 Table Of Content (TOC).

Benefits Of Using One Monitor

🔝 Go To TOC.

It is a common misconception that one monitor is not enough for coding. In reality, it can be very efficient.
Although, a lot of people have been using multiple monitors for years now. However, if you're a developer who really codes, there are some great benefits to using just one monitor for coding and development.

The first benefit is that you'll be able to save on desk space. With one monitor, you won't have to worry about clearing off your desk every time you need to code or work on something else. This will allow you to keep your desk clear of clutter and distractions so that it's easier for you to focus on your work.

The second benefit is that if the screen resolution is high enough, it will be easier for you to see the code in front of you without having to scroll back and forth between the two screens.

The third benefit is that your brain will be able to focus on the task at hand because the screen won't be divided into two parts. This means that your work will be easier to track and you'll have more clarity when you're working.I'm going to have one large monitor, but I might use a second monitor for something else (like a browser, for example).

The fourth benefit is that you'll be able to multitask better, because the screen will have a smaller "footprint." This means that the amount of space your monitor takes up will be less, and therefore you won't see two or three windows on one screen. This makes it easier to run multiple applications without constantly closing them. For example, you can have a spreadsheet open and a browser open, and you'll be able to move between them quickly.

The final benefit is that the screen will be easier to read because it'll have smaller borders. This means that your eyes won't need to travel as far across the screen when they're reading something on it, which will make it more comfortable for them.

Hidden Features of Using One Monitor

🔝 Go To TOC.

One of the main advantages of using one monitor instead of multiple monitors is that it forces you to focus on one task at a time.

Studies show that multitasking can actually reduce productivity and efficiency[0]. It can also lead to more stress and even physical pain[1]. Using a single monitor reduces the risk of this happening. This is one of the main benefits. It also puts less strain on your eyes when trying to view two screens at once, reducing eyestrain, headaches.

The benefits of using one monitor are numerous. One monitor is cheaper, more convenient, and more useful for multitasking. In addition, it's easier to share what you're doing with others on a single screen than on multiple screens. It's also easier to compare two objects on the same screen.

One of the major disadvantages of multiple monitors is that they are generally more difficult to move and set up than a single monitor, even after being adjusted by an expert. Another disadvantage though is that each screen must be adjusted for the correct position and size by an expert, which is more difficult than adjusting a single monitor.

In the next section, I am going to share a handful list of shortcuts i use on a daily basis that help me to easily navigate between applications and such.

Linux Keyboard Shortcuts

🔝 Go To TOC.
  • 🪟 + ↑: Maximize the app window.

  • 🪟 + →: Move the app window to the right half of the screen.

  • 🪟 + ↓: Minimize/Detach the app window.

  • 🪟 + ←: Move the app window to the left half of the screen.

  • ctrl + alt + ↑: Move to the previous workspace.

  • ctrl + alt + ↓: Move to the next workspace.

  • alt + tab: Move between applications.

Wrapping Up

🔝 Go To TOC.

This constitutes our guide on using a single monitor for your daily work. We have discussed the pros of using a single monitor, and the cons of multiple monitors. We also went over a list of keyboard shortcuts that help you easily manage your environment.

As always, this article is a gift to you, and you can share it with whomever you like or use it in any way that would be beneficial to your personal and professional development. By supporting this blog, you keep me motivated to publish high-quality content related to python. Thank you in advance for your ultimate support!

Happy coding, folks; see you in the next one.

Reference

🔝 Go To TOC.

[0] American Psychological Association, Multitasking undermines our efficiency, study suggests.

[1] Madore KP, Wagner AD. Multicosts of multitasking. Cerebrum. 2019;2019:cer-04-19.

[2] Ubuntu, Windows and workspaces.
item.author: wiseai

item.title: JWT（JSON Web Token）身份验证如何工作？ | How JWT (JSON Web Token) authentication works?
item.description:
它是紧凑的、可读的，并且由身份提供者 (IdP) 使用私钥/或公钥对进行数字签名。 因此代币的完整性和真实性可以被其他相关方验证。

使用 JWT 的目的不是隐藏数据，而是确保数据的真实性。 由于它是基于客户端的无状态会话，服务器不必完全依赖数据存储（数据库）来保存会话信息。

智威汤逊的结构

标题

算法可以是 HMAC、SHA256、RSA、HS256 或 RS256。

 { "typ": "JWT", "alg": "HS256" }

有效载荷

有效负载由称为声明的会话数据组成。 以下是我们可以使用的一些标准声明，

  1. 发行人(iss)
  2. 主题（子）
  3. 观众（审计）
  4. 到期时间（exp）
  5. 发行于 (iat)
 { "sub": "user10001", "iat": 1569302116 }

自定义声明也可以包含在声明集中。 使用自定义声明集时，

  • 不要将大量数据放入索赔集中。 声明集意味着紧凑。
  • 不要放敏感信息，因为 JWT 很容易被解码。
 { "sub": "user10001", "iat": 1569302116, "role": "admin", "user_id": "user10001" }

签名

签名是 JSON Web Token (JWT) 中最重要的部分。 签名是通过使用 Base64url 编码对标头和有效负载进行编码并将它们与句点分隔符连接起来来计算的。 然后将其提供给密码算法。

 // signature algorithm data = base64urlEncode( header ) + "." + base64urlEncode( payload ) signature = HMAC-SHA256( data, secret_salt )

因此，当标头或有效负载发生变化时，必须重新计算签名。 只有身份提供者（IdP）拥有私钥来计算签名，从而防止令牌被篡改。

这个怎么运作？

基本上，身份提供者（IdP）生成一个 JWT 证明用户身份，资源服务器使用秘密盐/公钥解码和验证令牌的真实性。

  1. 用户使用用户名和密码或 google/facebook 登录。
  2. 身份验证服务器验证凭据并发出使用秘密盐或私钥签名的 jwt。
  3. 用户的客户端通过在 HTTP 授权标头中传递 JWT 来使用 JWT 访问受保护的资源。
  4. 资源服务器然后使用秘密盐/公钥验证令牌的真实性。

安全

就像任何其他身份验证机制一样，JWT 也有自己的优点和缺点。

  • 必须使用 HTTPS 来保护授权标头。
  • 明确验证算法名称。 不要完全依赖 JWT 标头中提到的算法。 有一些基于标头的已知攻击，例如 algo none 攻击、标头剥离。
  • 从后端服务器撤销用户的会话是困难的。 由于 JWT 设置为自动过期，如果攻击者在令牌过期之前获得令牌，则会导致各种漏洞利用。 在您的服务器上构建令牌撤销列表以使令牌无效可能是缓解的最佳方法。
  • 如果 JWT 持久化在 cookie 上，我们需要创建 HttpOnly cookie。 这将限制第三方 javascripts 从 cookie 中读取 jwt 令牌。
  • 我们可以通过使用请求来源和特殊请求标头来缓解 CSRF。

如果您喜欢这篇文章，请订阅时事通讯并在Twitter上与我联系以获取我未来文章的更新。 ✅ ✅



原文:

JSON Web Token (JWT) is an open standard (RFC 7519) for securely transmitting information between parties as JSON object.

It is compact, readable and digitally signed using a private key/ or a public key pair by the Identity Provider(IdP). So the integrity and authenticity of the token can be verified by other parties involved.

The purpose of using JWT is not to hide data but to ensure the authenticity of the data. JWT is signed and encoded, not encrypted. 

JWT is a token based stateless authentication mechanism. Since it is a client-side based stateless session, server doesn't have to completely rely on a datastore(database) to save session information.

Structure of JWT

A JSON Web Token consists of 3 parts separated by a period.

Header

JWT header consists of token type and algorithm used for signing and encoding. Algorithms can be HMAC, SHA256, RSA, HS256 or RS256.

{ 
  "typ": "JWT",    
  "alg": "HS256"
}

Payload

Payload consists of the session data called as claims. Below are some of the the standard claims that we can use, 

  1. Issuer(iss)
  2. Subject (sub)
  3. Audience (aud)
  4. Expiration time (exp)
  5. Issued at (iat) 
{
"sub": "user10001",
"iat": 1569302116
}

Custom claims can also be included in the claim set. When using custom claim sets,

  • Do not put large data in claim sets. Claim sets meant to be compact. 
  • Do not put sensitive informations since, JWT can be decoded easily. 
{
"sub": "user10001",
"iat": 1569302116,
"role": "admin",
"user_id": "user10001"
}

Signature

Signature is most important part of a JSON Web Token(JWT). Signature is calculated by encoding the header and payload using Base64url Encoding and concatenating them with a period separator. Which is then given to the cryptographic algorithm.

// signature algorithm
data = base64urlEncode( header ) + "." + base64urlEncode( payload )
signature = HMAC-SHA256( data, secret_salt )

So when the header or payload changes, signature has to calculated again. Only the Identity Provider(IdP) has the private key to calculate the signature which prevents the tampering of token.

How it works?

Basically the identity provider(IdP) generates a JWT certifying user identity and Resource server decodes and verifies the authenticity of the token using secret salt / public key.

  1. User sign-in using username and password or google/facebook.
  2. Authentication server verifies the credentials and issues a jwt signed using either a secret salt or a private key.
  3. User's Client uses the JWT to access protected resources by passing the JWT in HTTP Authorization header.
  4. Resource server then verifies the authenticity of the token using the secret salt/ public key.

Security

Just like any other authentication mechanism, JWT also has its own pros and cons.

  • Must use HTTPS to secure the Authorization headers.
  • Validate algorithm name explicitly. Do not completely rely on the algorithm mentioned in the header of JWT. There are a few known attacks based on the header like algo none attack, header stripping.
  • Revoking the session of a user from backend server is difficult. Since a JWT is set to automatically expire, If an attacker gets the token before it expires It leads to various exploits. Building a token revocation list on your server to invalidate tokens could be best way to mitigate.
  • If JWT is persisted on cookies, we need to create HttpOnly cookie. This will restrict third party javascripts from reading jwt token from cookie.
  • XSS - backend servers must always sanitize user generated data.
  • CSRF  -  If JWT in persisted on cookies, CSRF attacks are possible. We can mitigate CSRF by using origin of request and special request headers.

If you like this article, subscribe to the newsletter and Connect with me on twitter to get updates on my future articles. ✅

item.title: Visual Studio 中基于 JavaScript 的 Excel Office 加载项 | JavaScript based Excel Office Add-in in Visual Studio
item.description:
如果您在 Office 加载项中构建 Office 应用程序，则可以使用这些技术 HTML、CSS 和 JavaScript，并与 Word、Excel、PowerPoint、OneNote、Project 和 Outlook 交互。 您的代码在 Office 中跨多个平台（包括 Windows、Mac、iPad 和浏览器）运行。

向 Office 客户端添加新功能：您可以将外部数据引入 Office、自动化 Office 文档、在 Office 客户端中公开第三方功能等。

  • 交互式对象可以嵌入到 Office 文档中：在 Office 插件中，您可以嵌入地图、图表和交互式可视化，以便用户可以添加到自己的 Excel 电子表格和 PowerPoint 演示文稿中。

    • 与使用 VBA、COM 或 VSTO 构建的加载项相比，Office 加载项具有以下优势：

    它是跨平台支持。 它是集中部署和分发。 管理员可以在整个组织的中心部署 Office 加载项。 它很容易通过 AppSource 访问。 通过将您的解决方案提交给 AppSource，您的解决方案可供广大受众使用。

    它基于标准的网络技术。 您将使用任何您喜欢的方式来构建 Office。

    您自己的 Web 应用程序：它需要托管在 Web 服务器或 Web 托管服务上，例如 Microsoft Azure。

    显现

    它是插件的指定设置和功能，例如：

    在加载项中，我们看到了显示名称、描述 ID、版本和默认区域设置。 您可以加载项与 Office 集成。

    您可以为加载项添加权限级别和数据访问要求。

    网络应用

    您可以为 Office 应用程序使用静态 HTML 页面构建。 您的 HTML 页面不与 Office 文档或任何其他 Internet 资源交互。 因此，您可以使用 Office 应用程序中使用的客户端和服务器端技术。

    阅读更多： Office 插件中的 Xml 清单是什么？

    扩展 Office 客户端并与之交互

    有两种方法可以扩展 Office 客户端的应用程序并与之交互。

    您可以使用任何 Office 应用程序扩展功能。
    您可以使用 Excel 或 PowerPoint Office 应用程序创建新对象。

    创建 Excel 任务窗格加载项

    先决条件

    下载具有最新 LTS 版本的 Node.js。
    您还可以下载最新版本的 Yeoman 和 Office 加载项的 Yeoman 生成器，并全局安装这些工具，并通过命令提示符运行这些命令。

    例子：

     npm install -g yo generator-office

    创建您的加载项项目

    您可以运行以下命令来使用 Yeoman 生成器创建加载项项目。

    例子：

    创建表

    您可以按照以下步骤创建表。 您可以测试您当前版本的 Excel 的加载项支持。 您可以将表格添加到工作表，用数据占用表格并对其进行格式化。

    编码加载项

    您的 Excel 加载项项目在您的 Visual Studio Code 中打开。

    您可以打开任务窗格文件夹中的 taskpane.html 文件。

    在 taskpane.html 文件中，您可以看到主标签元素并删除主标签和关闭主标签之间的所有行。

    您可以在打开主标签后添加以下行。

    例子：

     <button class="ms-button" id="createTable">Create Table</button>

    您可以打开任务窗格文件夹中的 taskpane.js 文件，taskpane.js 文件包含促进任务窗格和 Office 客户端应用程序之间交互的 Office JavaScript API 代码。

    您可以通过执行以下操作删除对运行按钮和 run() 方法的所有引用。

    删除行 document.getElementById(“run”).onclick = run; 删除整个 run() 函数。

    调用 Office.onReady 方法后，您会看到 if (info.host === Office.HostType.Excel )

    在第一部分中，您的代码确定用户的 Excel 版本是否支持包含将使用的所有 JavaScript API 的 Excel.js 版本。 您可以使用条件块的主体来隐藏或禁用会调用不受支持的 API 的 UI。 如果您允许用户仍然使用其 Excel 版本支持的加载项部分。

    在第二部分中，此代码为 createTable 按钮添加了一个事件处理程序。

    例子：

     if(!Office.context.requirements.isSetSupported('ExcelApi','1.7')){ console.log("This Javascript API is not available ") } document.getElementById("createTable").onclick= createTable;

    您可以在文件末尾添加以下函数。

    在 createTable 函数中，您可以传递 Excel.run 函数传递。 该函数不会立即执行。 今天查询。

    例子：

     function createTable() { Excel.run(function (context){ return context.sync(); }) .catch(function (error){ console.log("Error: "+ error); if(error instanceof OfficeExtension.Error) { console.log("Debug info: "+JSON.stringify(error.debugInfo)); } }); }

    首先，如果您传递四个值，那么您可以将包含 A1 列的表添加到 D1。 然后，定义表名。

    例子：

     var currentSheet = context.workbook.worksheets.getActiveWorksheet(); var employeeTable = currentSheet.tables.add("A1:D1"); employeeTable.name = "EmployeeTable";

    您可以使用 getHeaderRowRange() 方法添加员工的数据。

    例子

     employeeTable.getHeaderRowRange().values = [["EmployeeID","Name","Designation","DOB"]]; employeeTable.rows.add([ ["Emp1","Bhavin","Sales","12/1/1997"], ["Emp2","Hardik","Team Leader","10/12/1997"], ["Emp3","Gaurav","Project Manger","12/12/1997"], ["Emp4","Maulik","Sales","21/1/1997"], ["Emp5","Tejas","Project Manager","25/5/1997"], ["Emp6","Maulik","Team Leader","19/11/1997"] ]);

    之后，您可以使用 getRange() 方法调整列和行。

    例子：

     employeeTable.getRange().format.autofitColumns(); employeeTable.getRange().format.autofitRows();

    测试加载项

    如果您的项目在桌面应用程序中运行，那么您可以传递此命令。

    如果您的项目在 Web 浏览器中运行，那么您可以传递此命令

    结论

    在此博客中，我们创建了一个带有 Excel 加载项的表。 您还可以在 Excel 插件中实现排序表、过滤表和图表等，并可以在 Excel 插件中使用 JavaScript 探索更多功能。

    高级食欲信息 - Mar 31 3月31日



    原文:

    If you build an Office application in Office add-in you can use these technologies HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, and interact with Word, Excel, PowerPoint, OneNote, Project, and Outlook. Your code run in Office across multiple platforms, including Windows, Mac, iPad, and in a browser.

    The Office Add-ins can do anything that any webpage can do inside a browser. The Office Add-ins use the below platform in Visual Studio.

    • Add New Functionality to Office Clients : You can bring external data into Office, automate Office documents, expose third-party functionality in Office clients, and more.

    • The interactive objects can be embedded in Office documents : In the Office add-in, you can embed maps, charts, and interactive visualizations so the user can add to their own Excel spreadsheets and PowerPoint presentations.

    COM and VSTO Add-ins Different to Office-Add-ins?

    The COM or VSTO add-ins are earlier Office integration that runs only in Office on windows. The Office Add-ins don’t involve code that runs on the user’s device.

    The Office Add-ins provide below advantages over add-ins built using VBA, COM, or VSTO:

    It is cross-platform support. The Office Add-ins run in Office on the web, Windows, Mac, and iPad. It is a centralized deployment and distribution. The admins can deploy Office Add-ins in the center across an organization. It is easy to access via AppSource. Your solution is available to a broad audience by submitting it to AppSource.

    It is based on standard web technology. You will use any you like to build Office.

    Components of an Office Add-in

    There are basically two components in Office Add-in

    An XML manifest file : The manifest file defines the various setting, including how your add-in integrates with the office clients.

    Your own web application : it needs to be hosted on a web server, or web hosting service, such as Microsoft Azure.

    Manifest

    It is specified settings and capabilities of the add-in, such as:

    In the add-in’s we saw the display name, description ID, version, and default locale. You can add-in integrates with Office.

    You can add the permission level and data access requirements for the add-in.

    Web App

    You can use the static HTML page build for the Office application. Your HTML page doesn’t interact with either the Office document or any other Internet resource. Hence, you can use both client and server-side technologies used in Office applications.

    Read More: What Is Xml Manifest In Office Add-in?

    Extending and Interacting with Office Clients

    There are two ways you can extend and interact with Office client’s applications.

    You can extend functionality with any Office application.
    You can create a new object with Excel or PowerPoint Office application.

    Create an Excel Task Pane Add-in

    Prerequisites

    Download Node.js with the latest LTS version.
    You can also download the latest version of Yeoman and the Yeoman generator for Office Add-ins, and install these tools globally, and run these commands via the command prompt.

    Example:

        npm install -g yo generator-office

    Create Your Add-In Project

    You can run the following command to create an add-in project using the Yeoman generator.

    Example:

    Create a Table

    You can follow the steps to create a table. You can test your add-in supports for your current version of Excel. You can add a table to the worksheet, occupy the table with data, and format it.

    Code the Add-In

    Your Excel add-in project open in your Visual Studio Code.

    You can open the taskpane.html file in the task pane folder.

    In taskpane.html file, you can see main tag element and delete all lines between main tag and closing main tag.

    You can add the below line after opening the main tag.

    Example:

      <button class="ms-button" id="createTable">Create Table</button>

    You can open the taskpane.js file in the task pane folder, the taskpane.js file contains the Office JavaScript API code that facilities interaction between the task pane and the Office client application.

    You can remove all references to the run button and the run () method by doing the following.

    Delete the line document.getElementById(“run”).onclick = run; Delete the entire run () function.

    After you can call Office.onReady method, you see the if (info.host === Office.HostType.Excel )

    In the first part, your code determines whether the user’s version of Excel supports a version of Excel.js that includes all the JavaScript APIs will use. You can use the body of the conditional block to hide or disable the UI that would call unsupported APIs. If you enable to the user to still make use of the parts of the add-in that are supported by their version of Excel.

    In the second part, this code adds an event handler for the createTable button.

    Example:

      if(!Office.context.requirements.isSetSupported('ExcelApi','1.7')){
    console.log("This Javascript API is not available ")
  }
document.getElementById("createTable").onclick= createTable;

    You can add the following function to the end of the file.

    In the createTable function you can pass the Excel.run function pass. This function will not execute immediately. Return context.sync() method sends all queued commands to Excel for execution.

    The catch block is followed to Excel.run() function.

    One Destination for Excel Add-in Development Solutions? Enquire Today.

    Example:

        function createTable() {
      Excel.run(function (context){
        return context.sync();
      })
      .catch(function (error){
        console.log("Error: "+ error);
        if(error instanceof OfficeExtension.Error) {
          console.log("Debug info: "+JSON.stringify(error.debugInfo));
        }
        });
    }

    First of all, if you pass four values so you can add the table with column A1 to D1. Then, you define the table name.

    Example:

        var currentSheet = context.workbook.worksheets.getActiveWorksheet();
    var employeeTable = currentSheet.tables.add("A1:D1");
employeeTable.name = "EmployeeTable";

    You can add the data of employee with getHeaderRowRange() method.

    Example

        employeeTable.getHeaderRowRange().values = [["EmployeeID","Name","Designation","DOB"]];
    employeeTable.rows.add([
      ["Emp1","Bhavin","Sales","12/1/1997"],
      ["Emp2","Hardik","Team Leader","10/12/1997"],
      ["Emp3","Gaurav","Project Manger","12/12/1997"],
      ["Emp4","Maulik","Sales","21/1/1997"],
      ["Emp5","Tejas","Project Manager","25/5/1997"],
      ["Emp6","Maulik","Team Leader","19/11/1997"]
  ]);

    After that, you can fit the columns and rows with getRange() method.

    Example:

        employeeTable.getRange().format.autofitColumns();
    employeeTable.getRange().format.autofitRows();

    Test the Add-In

    If your project runs in a desktop application so you can pass this command.

    If your project run in a web browser so you can pass this command

    Conclusion

    In this blog, we have created a table with an Excel add-in. You can also implement sort table, filter table, and charts, etc. in Excel add-in and can explore more features with JavaScript in Excel add-in.

    advancedappetiteinfo - Mar 31

item.title: 使用 Open Movie Database API 构建电影信息应用程序。 | Building a movie-info application using the Open Movie Database API.
item.description:
希望皮特

发表于让我们首先看一下**API **这个词本身。

让我们首先分解单词并单独理解所有组件

应用程序 - 我们想要与之通信的应用程序

编程 - 控制或指挥

接口 - 一种交流方式 - （如家中的总机）
简而言之，API 的功能类似于虚拟中间人，将信息从一个界面（如移动应用程序）传递到另一个界面。 用户界面
用户界面 (UI) 是设备中人机交互和通信的点。 这可以包括显示屏、键盘、鼠标和桌面外观。 它也是用户与应用程序或网站交互的方式。

执行良好的用户界面通过对比鲜明的视觉效果、简洁的设计和响应能力，促进用户与程序、应用程序或机器之间的有效交互。

应用程序编程接口在这个项目中，我使用了 Open Movie Database API。 现在让我们回到您阅读本文的初衷——在 JavaScript 中使用 API。

这个名称中最重要的部分是“接口”，因为 API 本质上是为您与程序对话。

用户：提出请求的人

  • 客户端：向服务器发送请求的计算机
  • 服务器：响应请求的计算机

    • 服务器拥有数据并设置语言，而客户端使用该语言向服务器请求信息。

    将此视为向服务器添加信息，例如创建新条目

  • 大多数 API 都需要 API 密钥。 找到要使用的 API 后，请查看文档以了解访问要求。 大多数 API 会要求您完成身份验证，例如使用您的 Google 帐户登录。 在访问 API 时，您将获得一个唯一的字母和数字字符串，而不是每次都添加您的电子邮件和密码。

    所以下一步是从 API 中获取数据，这就是我们将使用 fetch 函数告诉浏览器执行的操作。fetch 函数返回一个 promise，它可以解析响应详细信息或在发生错误时被拒绝。

    总体而言，API 请求看起来与普通浏览器 URL 没有太大区别，但返回的数据将采用计算机易于阅读的形式。

    部署
    构建网络应用程序后，我将其托管在 gh-pages 上。起初它并不成功，因为我遇到了被阻止的混合内容。
    混合内容警告表明您通过 HTTPS 访问的网页存在问题。 由于 HTTPS 连接是安全的，并且网页的源代码正在使用不安全的 HTTP 协议而不是 HTTPS 拉取其他资源。 您的 Web 浏览器的地址栏会显示您已使用 HTTPS 连接，但该页面还在后台使用不安全的 HTTP 协议加载资源。 为确保您知道您正在使用的网页并不完全安全，浏览器会显示一条警告，指出该页面同时包含 HTTPS 和 HTTP 内容——即混合内容。

    解决方案
    将以下标签放入

    文档的部分以强制浏览器阻止混合内容

    结论
    在 JavaScript 应用中实现 OMDb-Search 的步骤可以总结如下：

    • 使用 OMDb API 来获取与您的搜索查询 (JSON) 匹配的前 10 个结果。
    • 创建一个带有一个输入字段的界面，用于按标题搜索电影。
    • 实时呈现结果并在用户键入搜索查询时更新界面。
    • 如果搜索查询没有结果，则显示 OMDb API 错误
    • 显示结果后，用户可以单击任何特定电影以查看其详细信息。
    • 对于特定电影，详细信息视图会显示更多电影信息。

    可以在此处访问应用程序的完整代码。

    总督算法工程 - Apr 24 4月24日



    • 原文:

    Hope Pete

    Posted on Apr 25

    Let us first look at the word **API **itself.

    APIs stands for Application Programming Interface
    Lets first break up the word and understand all components individually

    Application - An application that we are wanting to communicate with

    Programming - Controlling or commanding

    Interface - A way to communicate - (like switchboard at homes)
    Put simply, an API functions like a virtual middleman, relaying information from one interface, like a mobile app, to another. APIs connect different parts of a software platform to ensure that information ends up in the right place.

    User Interface
    The user interface (UI) is the point of human-computer interaction and communication in a device. This can include display screens, keyboards, a mouse and the appearance of a desktop. It is also the way through which a user interacts with an application or a website.

    UI is important to meet user expectations and support the effective functionality of your site. A well-executed user interface facilitates effective interaction between the user and the program, app or machine through contrasting visuals, clean design and responsiveness.

    Application Programming Interface In this project i used the Open Movie Database API. Now let's come back to the point why you are reading this article in the first place - To use an API in JavaScript.

    The most important part of this name is “interface,” because an API essentially talks to a program for you.

    APIs consist of three parts:

    • User: the person who makes a request
    • Client: the computer that sends the request to the server
    • Server: the computer that responds to the request

    An API call is a request that the client computer sends to the server.
    An API is how two computers talk to each other. The server has the data and sets the language, while the client uses that language to ask for information from the server.

    There are four types of actions an API can take:

    • GET: requests data from a server — can be status or specifics (like last_name)
    • POST: sends changes from the client to the server; think of this as adding information to the server, like making a new entry
    • PUT: revises or adds to existing information
    • DELETE: deletes existing information

    Most APIs require an API key. Once you find an API you want to play with, look in the documentation for access requirements. Most APIs will ask you to complete an identity verification, like signing in with your Google account. You’ll get a unique string of letters and numbers to use when accessing the API, instead of just adding your email and password every time.

    So the next step would be to fetch the data from API, so that is what we will tell the browser to do using the fetch function.The fetch function returns a promise which can resolve to response details or be rejected if some error occurs.

    Overall, an API request doesn’t look that much different from a normal browser URL, but the returned data will be in a form that’s easy for computers to read.

    Deployment
    After building the web app,I hosted it on gh-pages.At first it wasn't successful as I encountered blocked mixed content.
    Mixed content warnings indicate a problem with a web page you’re accessing over HTTPS. Since the HTTPS connection is secure, and the web page’s source code is pulling in other resources with the insecure HTTP protocol, not HTTPS. Your web browser’s address bar will say you’re connected with HTTPS, but the page is also loading resources with the insecure HTTP protocol in the background. To ensure you know that the web page you’re using isn’t completely secure, browsers display a warning saying that the page has both HTTPS and HTTP content — mixed content, in other words.

    Solution
    Put the below tag into the

    section of your document to force the browser to block the mixed content

    *
    *

    Conclusion
    Steps of implementing OMDb-Search in JavaScript application can be summarized as follows:

    • Consume the OMDb API to fetch the top 10 results matching your search query (JSON).
    • Create an interface with one input field to be used to search for movies by title.
    • Present the results in real-time and update the interface as the user types his search query.
    • Displays the OMDb API error if there is no result for the search query
    • Once the results are displayed allowing the user to click on any particular movie to view its details.
    • For a particular movie, the details view display with more movie information.

    The full code for the application can be accessed here.

    Doge Algo Engineering - Apr 24
item.author: achiengy

item.title: 在 Expedia 的 2 年教会了我什么 | What 2 years at Expedia Taught Me
item.description:
嘿！ 有一阵子了。 事实上，距离我上一篇博文已有一年多的时间了。 从那以后，发生了很多事情，我有很多时间来反思这一切！ 这是另一天的另一篇文章，但今天我想谈谈我在 Expedia Group 工作的经历以及我学到的一些教训。

我于 2017 年秋天在密歇根州安娜堡加入了 Expedia。他们的办公室（包括我自己在内的许多人都不知道存在）最初是一家名为Mobiata的年轻创业公司，该公司很早就被收购了。 这就是我们亲切地称呼它的 Expedia A2 的由来。 我非常喜欢在这里工作； 这确实是一份梦寐以求的工作。 并不是说我对旅​​行一无所知，而是与我一起工作的每个人都很关心。 那时我不知道，但从团队环境到我们构建的产品，它产生了天壤之别。

得到教训

面试

与我当时正在经历的其他面试相比，面试过程非常令人耳目一新。 在最初的电话采访中，我出现了焦虑症，导致了极度的脑雾。 我被问及如何在guard声明中快速展开选项并完全空白，具有讽刺意味的是，这是我最喜欢的。 面试官理解并帮助引导我找到答案，这让我能够强调我是如何使用该语句来清理我最近正在处理的代码库中的嵌套 if 语句的。 其他采访会继续进行并将我注销。 最后一个阶段是半天的面对面采访，与团队、经理会面，然后进行一轮白板、现场编码会议和团队午餐。 时间很长，但它教会了我关于面试的最有力的一课，从那以后我应用于每个角色。 一路走来，我从不孤单。 采访我的人都和我一起解决了这个问题。 如果我被卡住了，我可以提出问题或澄清。 如果我想要他们的想法，我只是问。 每一步都明确了期望。 这是一个配对练习； 我们想知道您如何与我们合作，而不仅仅是您一个人。 是的，需要解决问题，但这次面试是我经历的第一个反映团队环境的过程。

一个团体

我们所做的一切都是作为一个团队。 我选择强调这个口头禅，因为它引起了我的共鸣，并且在领导链上很明显。 我不仅感觉与我的领导和他的经理有联系，而且我们的福利很棒，我们的假期很棒，而且无论他们的一名员工生活如何，他们似乎都支持你。

其中一项福利是志愿者日，办公室内的任何人都可以参加当地的志愿者活动以回馈社区。 一年我们在当地公园清理入侵物种，接下来是为有需要的人分类农产品。 这种前瞻性方法令人耳目一新，而不是大多数公司提供的典型的不干涉货币匹配。

当您的团队和您的公司为您的健康、幸福和社区投资时，每天上班和全力以赴成为一个简单的选择。 错误发生

虽然我真的很喜欢我在 Expedia 的时光，但没有什么是完美的。 有一个“假设积极意图”的价值。 虽然这对我来说是新的，但它绝对有意义。 我在这种模式下工作得越多，与其他人谈论他们的生活经历，我就越意识到它弊大于利。 虽然我会很快再次与我的许多同事一起工作，但很明显，有些人会依靠这个价值来帮助他们摆脱错误的选择，无论是否有意识。 行为模式清楚地表明，这是他们留在公司的生存方式。 作为实践中这种危害的一个例子，移动领域的一位突出领导者习惯于 my-way 或 high-way。 他们会倾听，但也如此专注于他们的思考过程，以至于从来没有真正感觉到相反的想法被考虑在内。 在更大的团队内部进行了对话，以便在您的更改上线之前进行更改，这绝不是一个好兆头。 我在这里最大的教训是，虽然看起来某事或某人有良好的意图，但要寻找模式。 作为一家企业，不断获得有关您的实践和价值观的反馈。 当有人说他们正因某件事而受到伤害时，请倾听，然后采取行动加以改善。

保持谦虚

为 Expedia 工作是令人兴奋的，并且足以让我们涉足“大型科技”领域，这仍然是一次很好的体验。 我真的很喜欢我们所做的工作、我们构建的产品以及我们培养的团队环境。 没想到一眨眼就变了。

在 COVID-19 撕裂旅游业之前，Expedia 在 2020 年初经历了大规模裁员。 虽然许多优秀的员工被解雇了，但我们整个下（小）办公室也是如此。 尽管我们是顶级制作团队之一，并且在领导链上获得了很好的评价，但我们并不是无敌的。 这对我们来说没有意义，但这只是一个数字游戏。 虽然我为失去一份出色的工作而感到悲痛，但在看到接下来的一年给世界带来的一切之后，我很感激它以它的方式发生。 没有这个，我就不会在下一份工作中获得高级职位。 如果没有这个，我就不会离开科技行业并与职业教练和人才顾问联系，看看我有什么未来。 如果没有这些偶然事件，我不会成为今天的我，领导一个移动团队并为他们提供我所享受的同样的支持和领导。



原文:

Hey there! It's been a while. As a matter of fact, over a year since my last blog post. Since then, a lot has happened, and I've had a lot of time to reflect on it all! That's another post for another day, but today I want to talk about my experience working at Expedia Group and some of the lessons I learned.

I joined Expedia in Ann Arbor, Mi, in the fall of 2017. Their office (which many, including myself, didn't know existed) began as a young startup called Mobiata that had been acquired early on. That's how Expedia A2, as we lovingly called it, came to be. I thoroughly enjoyed working here; it was indeed a dream job. Not that I'm head over heels about travel, but every person I worked with cared. I didn't know it then, but it made a world of difference from the team environment to the product we built.

Lessons Learned

Interviewing

The interview process was exceptionally refreshing compared to the other interviews I was going through at the time. During the initial phone interview, I had an anxiety attack resulting in extreme brain fog. I was asked about unwrapping optionals in swift and completely blanked on the guard statement, which was ironically my favorite. The interviewer understood and helped guide me to the answer, which allowed me to highlight how I've used the statement to clean up nested if statements in a codebase I was working on recently. Other interviews would have just moved on and written me off. The last phase was a half-day in-person interview to meet the team, the manager, then go through a round of whiteboarding, a live coding session and team lunch. It was long, but it taught me the most powerful lesson about interviewing that I've applied to every role since. Every step of the way, I was never alone. Whoever was interviewing me worked through the problem with me. If I was stuck, I could ask questions or clarification. If I wanted their thoughts, I just asked. Expectations were made clear every step of the way. This is a pairing exercise; we want to know how you work with us, not just on your own. Yes, problem-solving was needed, but this interview was the first process I went through that reflected the team environment.

One Team

"One Team" was a mantra used to drive home that you're not alone in success or failure. Everything we did was as a team. I chose to highlight this mantra because it resonated with me and was evident up the leadership chain. Not only did I feel connected to my leader and his manager, but our benefits were great, our leave was terrific, and no matter what life throws at one of their employees, it seemed like they had your back.

"One Team" also echoed out into the community. One of the perks was a volunteer day, where anyone within the office could attend a local volunteer event to give back to the community. One year we cleaned up invasive species in a local park, and the next was sorting produce for people in need. This forward approach was a refreshing take rather than the typical hands-off monetary match most corporations offer.

When your team and your company are invested in your health, happiness, and community, it makes going to work and putting your best foot forward every day an easy choice. It's been two years since Expedia A2 closed, and I still stay in touch with almost everyone.

Mistakes Happen

While I truly loved my time at Expedia, nothing is perfect. There was a value of "assume positive intent". While it was new to me, it made absolute sense. The more I worked under this model and talked with other people about their lived experiences, the more I realized it was doing more harm than good. While I'd work with many of my co-workers again in a heartbeat, it became evident that some would count on this value to bail them out of their bad choices, conscious or not. The pattern of behaviors made it clear that this was their survival method of staying with the company. As an example of this harm in practice, a prominent lead in the mobile space had a habit of my-way or the high-way. They would listen but were also so wrapped up in their thought process that it never really felt like the opposing thoughts were considered. There were conversations within the greater team to get your changes in before they came online, which is never a good sign. My biggest lesson here is while it may seem like something or someone has good intentions, look for the patterns. As a business, constantly get feedback on your practices and values. When someone says they are experiencing harm from something in place, listen, then act to improve it.

Stay Humble

Working for Expedia was exciting and enough of a toe-dip into the "Big Tech" scene that it was still a good experience. I truly loved the work we did, the product we built, and the team environment we fostered. I didn't consider that it could all change within a blink of an eye.

Expedia went through mass layoffs early in 2020 before COVID-19 tore apart the travel industry. While many excellent staff members were laid off, so was our entire off (small) office. Despite being one of the top-producing teams, and getting great reviews up the leadership chain, we weren't invincible. It didn't make sense to us, but it was just a numbers game. While I mourned the loss of a fantastic job, after seeing what the following year(s) gave the world, I'm thankful it happened the way it did. Without that, I wouldn't have gone for a senior title at my next job. Without that, I wouldn't have been one insincere review away from leaving tech and reaching out to a career coach and talent advisor to see what future I had. I wouldn't be where I am today without those happenstances, leading a mobile team and providing them with the same support and leadership I've enjoyed.
item.author: kaydacode

item.title: 为什么迁移到 Oracle 云应用程序？ | Why Migrate To Oracle Cloud Applications?
item.description:
缓慢而稳定地，世界各地的公司开始意识到云的力量。 由于技术继续以极快的速度转型，不断更新定制的本地基础设施和使用独立业务应用程序运行的传统方法可能行不通。 为了更快地改进并为未来做好准备，公司应努力使用完全集成的基于云的企业软件实现运营现代化，并计划将Oracle 迁移到云。

高度依赖资源的本地应用程序无法跟上企业不断变化的需求。 借助 Oracle 云应用程序，组织可以访问现代技术。

为了执行 Oracle 到云的无缝迁移，Oracle 云基础设施提供了各种工具和架构，允许企业从本地应用程序迁移到云。 这样的事情允许利用改进的自动化和内置的安全性来减少几种威胁，最终促进卓越的迁移和经济性。

这样的事情促进了集成控制和治理、行业领先的可扩展性和可用性以及端到端 SLA 支持的可靠性。 此外，甲骨文还鼓励融合机器学习（ML）、人工智能、区块链、物联网（IoT）和人机界面等技术。

提高性能的潜力。
这种差异化使企业能够享受可预测的性能和客户隔离。

此外，事实证明，自主工作负载在旨在提供高可用性、低延迟、弹性和一致性能的基础架构上效果最佳。 这表明您的所有业务数据都存储在 Oracle 的对象存储中，并且会自动复制大量副本，从而提供高可用性和弹性。 此外，Oracle 的自主自我修复功能可确保所有业务数据保持健康，员工始终可以访问最后一个已知的可靠版本。

即兴安全
在 Oracle 云基础设施中，安全性是最重要的。 通过执行 oracle 迁移到云，企业可以享受安全的设计保护，例如数据安全、客户隔离、内部威胁检测和高度自动化的威胁修复。 此外，它还有助于将您的数据、代码和资源与管理机器区分开来——有助于防止攻击者在云中窃取或操纵数据。

它包含多层防御，包括专用防火墙、DDoS 和旨在主动检测和阻止威胁的加密。 当用户活动显示出更高级别的风险时，组织可以在新边界创建身份并使用自适应身份验证系统来进一步增强安全性。 此外，甲骨文拥有业界最广泛的安全服务组合之一，涵盖云和本地解决方案

关于公司
在 Opkey，我们是 Oracle 云迁移专家； 无论您是从内部部署还是从经典 Oracle 云基础设施迁移。 此外，我们还提供无代码连续测试自动化平台，易于使用且为变化而构建。

标题：为什么要迁移到 Oracle 云应用程序？

描述：在本博客中，我们将研究为什么要迁移到 Oracle 云应用程序？



原文:

Slowly but steadily, companies around the world are beginning to realize the power of the cloud. Since technology continues to transform at breakneck speed, the traditional methods of continuously updating customized on-premises infrastructure and operating with independent business applications may not work. To improve faster and prepare for the future, companies should work toward modernizing their operations with completely integrated, cloud-based enterprise software and plan toward oracle migration to cloud.

Highly resource-dependent, on-premises applications are unable to keep pace with the ever-evolving needs of businesses. With oracle cloud applications, organizations get access to modern technologies.

Oracle Offers A Second-Generation Cloud.
For performing seamless oracle migration to cloud, oracle cloud infrastructure offers various tools and architecture that allows businesses to move from on-premise applications to the cloud. Such a thing allows to leverage improved automation and built-in security to reduce several threats, ultimately promoting superior migration and economics.

Oracle Cloud Infrastructure is built for change and to improvise various business operations. Such a thing promotes integrated control and governance, industry-leading scalability and availability, and reliability supported by end-to-end SLAs. In addition, Oracle also encourages merging technologies such as machine learning (ML), AI, blockchain, the Internet of Things (IoT), and human interfaces.

Potential To Improve Performance.
Oracle migration to cloud allows businesses to utilize a wide range of capabilities unparalleled in the industry – from its second-generation platform to remote direct memory access (RDMA). Such differentiation allows businesses to enjoy both predictable performance and customer isolation.

Cloud Infrastructure offered by Oracle also allows organizations to utilize modern innovations such as Oracle Autonomous Transaction Processing and Oracle Autonomous Data Warehouse. Moreover, it has been proved that autonomous workload works best on an infrastructure that is designed to offer high availability, low latency, resiliency, and consistent performance. This indicates all your businesses’ data is stored in Oracle’s object storage, and numerous copies are automatically replicated – offering high availability and resiliency. In addition, Oracle’s autonomous self-repairing capabilities ensure that all the businesses data remain healthy and employees can always access the last-known reliable version.

Improvised Security
Security is given utmost importance within Oracle Cloud Infrastructure. By performing oracle migration to cloud, businesses can enjoy secure by design protection such as data security, customer isolation, internal-threat detection, and highly automated threat remediation. Oracle cloud infrastructure allows organizations to isolate compute and network resources to ensure that businesses’ data and traffic remain protected from users with malicious intent. In addition, it also helps distinguish your data, code, and resources from management machines – helping prevent attackers from stealing or data manipulation in the cloud.

Oracle Cloud also provides a limited attack surface with the help of granular customer isolation. It comprises numerous layers of defense with dedicated firewalls, DDoS, and encryption that is designed to detect and stop threats proactively. Organizations can create identity at the new perimeter and use adaptive authentication systems to further enhance security when a user activity displays higher levels of risk. Moreover, Oracle boasts one of the industry’s broadest portfolios of security services, spanning cloud and on-premise solutions

About The Company
At Opkey, we are Oracle Cloud Migration experts; whether you’re migrating from on-premise or from classic Oracle Cloud Infrastructure. In addition, we also offer no code continuous test automation platform that is easy to use and built for change.

Title: Why Migrate To Oracle Cloud Applications?

Description: In this blog, we will study why migrate to Oracle Cloud Applications?

item.title: 混音和动态路由 | Remix and dynamic routes
item.description:
现在我们在 Remix 中有我们的帖子概览页面，让我们看看如何从这些数据中添加各个页面。

我们已经能够点击概览上的帖子并转到每个相应的页面，例如：

  • posts/post-1
  • posts/post-2

但是现在，它们不存在，所以让我们看看如何让它们动态工作。

如果您想继续学习，请下载此 GitHub 存储库作为您的起点。

快速回顾

我们在上一篇文章中添加了一个帖子模型，作为我们的数据源。 你可以在这里找到它： app/models/post.server.ts 。

目前，它只是输出一个帖子数组，但最终，我们将努力从外部源加载。

为了在概览页面上加载这些帖子，我们使用了 Remix 中内置的useLoaderData钩子。

我们将应用相同的概念来创建我们的动态页面。
像许多这些现代框架一样，我们可以选择创建一个可以用作动态文件的文件。

在 Remix 中，我们使用美元符号来使文件动态化。
创建动态发布文件： app/routes/posts/$slug.tsx 。

 import { json , LoaderFunction } from ' @remix-run/node ' ; import { useLoaderData } from ' @remix-run/react ' ; import { getPost } from ' ~/models/post.server ' ; type LoaderData = { post : Awaited < ReturnType < typeof getPost >> , }; export const loader : LoaderFunction = async ({ params }) => { return json ({ post : await getPost ( params . slug ), }); };

现在，这将根据 slug 找到与此参数匹配的帖子。

然而这个getPost函数还不存在，所以让我们打开我们的model并快速创建它。

 export async function getPost ( slug : string | undefined ): Promise < Post > { const posts = await getPosts (); return posts . filter (( post ) => post . slug === slug )[ 0 ]; }

如您所见，这是基于检索所有帖子的现有函数，但我们将其限制为基于 slug 进行匹配。

然后，例如，我们可以返回一些 HTML 并呈现我们的帖子标题。

 export default function PostSlug () { const { post } = useLoaderData () as LoaderData ; return ( < main className = " mx-auto max-w-4xl " > < h1 className = " my-6 border-b-2 text-center text-3xl " > The post title : { post . title } < /h1 > < /main > ); }

让我们尝试一下，看看会发生什么。

是的，我们做到了。
我们现在已经在 Remix 中管理了我们的动态路线。

你可以在GitHub 上找到完整的代码。

感谢您的阅读，让我们联系吧！

感谢您阅读我的博客。 随时订阅我的电子邮件通讯并在Facebook或Twitter 上联系



原文:

Now that we have our post overview page in Remix let's see how we can add the individual pages from this data.

We are already able to click on the posts on the overview and go to each respective page like:

  • posts/post-1
  • posts/post-2

But for now, they are non-existing, so let's see how we can make those work dynamically.

If you would like to follow along, download this GitHub repo as your starting point.

A quick recap

We've added a posts model in the previous article, which acts as our data source. You can find it here: app/models/post.server.ts.

For now, it simply outputs an array of posts, but eventually, we'll work to make this load from an external source.

To load these posts on the overview page, we use the useLoaderData hook built into Remix.

We are going to apply the same concept to create our dynamic pages.
Like many of these modern frameworks, we have an option to create one file that can serve as a dynamic file.

In Remix, we use a dollar sign to make a file dynamic.
Create the dynamic post file: app/routes/posts/$slug.tsx.

import { json, LoaderFunction } from '@remix-run/node';
import { useLoaderData } from '@remix-run/react';
import { getPost } from '~/models/post.server'; type LoaderData = { post: Awaited<ReturnType<typeof getPost>>,
}; export const loader: LoaderFunction = async ({ params }) => { return json({ post: await getPost(params.slug), });
};

This will now find the post matching this param based on the slug.

However this getPost function doesn't exist yet, so let's open our model and create it quickly.

export async function getPost(slug: string | undefined): Promise<Post> { const posts = await getPosts(); return posts.filter((post) => post.slug === slug)[0];
}

As you can see, this is based on the existing function that retrieves all the posts, but we limit it to match based on the slug.

And then, we can return some HTML and render our post title, for instance.

export default function PostSlug() { const { post } = useLoaderData() as LoaderData; return ( <main className="mx-auto max-w-4xl"> <h1 className="my-6 border-b-2 text-center text-3xl"> The post title: {post.title} </h1>
 </main>
 );
}

Let's try it out and see what happens.

Yes, we did it.
We now have managed our dynamic routes in Remix.

You can find the completed code on GitHub.

Thank you for reading, and let's connect!

Thank you for reading my blog. Feel free to subscribe to my email newsletter and connect on Facebook or Twitter

item.title: Python 如何加强网络安全？ | How Python is fortifying Cybersecurity?
item.description:
网络攻击是一个国际问题，因为高调的违规行为引起了真正的担忧。 最近黑客的攻击每 39 秒发生一次。 最近的 Web 开发统计数据表明，迄今为止，近 64% 的公司都面临过 Web 攻击。 编程正在成为网络安全不可分割的一部分，定制 Web 应用程序开发公司更愿意聘请专门的开发人员来解决在 Python 环境中导航的问题。 什么是网络攻击？

网络攻击是针对计算机信息系统、计算机网络、基础设施或个人计算机设备的任何攻击性策略。 网络攻击是一个组织或个人的恶意和故意尝试破坏另一个组织或个人的信息系统。 网络攻击者使用恶意代码破坏数据，从而导致网络犯罪。

为了减少这些威胁，美国的Web 应用程序开发公司主要确保云存储数据库不应该被外人访问。 此外，为了增强安全性，网络安全专业人员可以确保安全的软件开发生命周期，以防止不干净的代码。 由于其用途广泛且曲线较短，因此向网络安全团队的新人推荐。

它是一种服务器端脚本语言，具有一系列应用程序，已在数千个安全项目中使用。 它是数据科学、Web 开发、数学计算以及更重要的网络安全等众多领域的编码人员的首选语言。

对开发者的好处：

与 C 或 Java 相比，Python 执行相同任务所需的代码要少得多。 它为新手提供了更短的学习曲线，因为它是一种简单的编程语言。

调试变得简单：
此外，简单的设计和任务完成的简易性等价地增加了它的可读性。 增加的易读性也使调试代码变得非常简单。 这使得入门级编码器的故障排除变得容易，从而增加了周转时间和效率。

开源编程语言：
与 Linux 是一种开源操作系统类似，Python 是作为一种开源编程语言开发的。 开源自然有它的好处。 作为一种开源编程语言，它一直受到强大的开发人员社区的支持、改进和升级。 使用该语言是免费的，具有灵活性和市场上可用的大量信息的优势。

自主内存管理：
另一个容易被忽略的好处是 Python 具有内置的自动内存管理功能。 这减轻了程序员或用户在内存管理问题上的负担，因为它包括缓存、内存分配和分段等因素。

网络安全专业人员的好处

加快进程：
由于其学习曲线较短，技术背景较少的新手可以快速获得结果。 由于易于使用，这种编程语言甚至受到经验丰富的程序员或网络安全专业人员的青睐，因为代码可以在瞬间执行和实施。

可以快速组建团队：
即使团队由没有编码背景的成员组成，网络安全团队也可以为项目提供更快的周转时间。 团队理解的复杂性会随着 Java 等更难的语言的使用而增加，从而降低团队的效率。 一个广泛的 Python 库提供了用于网络安全的工具的可用性：
由于本质上是开源的，Python 的广泛库不断更新，可供所有人使用。 网络安全专业人员不会重新发明具有常见任务的工具，并且在许多情况下可以快速搜索网络安全分析或渗透测试工具。

网络安全中的多种用途：
借助对 Python 的深入理解和一般编程理论，网络安全专业人员可以使用 Python 代码完成任何事情。 例如，Python 被广泛用于恶意软件分析、数据包的发送和解码、主机发现、端口扫描、访问服务器和网络扫描。 在 Python 中快速开发脚本：
在短时间内为网络安全专业人员提供有效且易于实施和调试的解决方案的简单代码本身就是一个巨大的好处。 代码中的错误非常容易检测和修复，这意味着网络安全专业人员可以解决快速出现的问题并减少网络安全之旅中的挑战。

地理位置提取：
使用 Python 和 Google API 可以轻松提取任何 IP 地址的实时位置。 网络安全专业人员可以使用此工具来跟踪网络攻击的起源地，这非常有用，原因有很多。 该工具不仅具有跟踪 IP 地址的优势，还可以用于阻止想要破坏信息系统的恶意 IP 地址。

在不使用任何第三方工具且仅使用 Python 作为基础编程语言的情况下，可以完成以下操作：
结论：
近年来，随着网络攻击的日益严重和技术进步，网络安全已成为强制性要求。 它执行多种功能，例如恶意软件分析、渗透测试和扫描功能。 通过网络安全保护您的 Web 应用程序或网站是世界进行数字化改造的最佳选择。

网络专业人员确保网站或网络应用程序的安全和更好的性能，以及对可能影响您的数字存在的任何网络攻击的准备就绪响应。 您可以与我们联系，聘请专门的开发人员来保护您的在线形象。

查看原文： Python 网络安全



原文:

Cyber-attacks are an international issue, as breaches of high-profiles have given rise to genuine concerns. Hackers’ attacks in recent times occur every 39 seconds. Recent web development statistics suggest nearly 64% of companies have faced web attacks to date. Programming is becoming an integral part of cybersecurity and custom web application development company prefer to hire dedicated developers to tackle the if’s and but’s of navigating through the Python environment. Python programming language has been around for quite some time now in the industry, gaining more attention from cybersecurity professionals.

What are Cyber Attacks?

A cyberattack is any offensive maneuverer that targets computer information systems, computer networks, infrastructures, or personal computer devices. Cyber-attacks are malicious and intentional attempts by an organisation or individual to breach the information system of another organisation or individual. Cyber attackers use malicious code to compromise data which leads to cybercrime.

To lessen these threats, web application development companies in USA make sure primarily the cloud storage database should be inaccessible to outsiders. Furthermore, to enhance security, cybersecurity professionals can ensure a secure software development lifecycle for the prevention of unclean codes. Python comes in handy in such scenarios, mainly for the multitude of benefits it provides.

Is Python useful for cybersecurity?

Python is a critical programming language for cybersecurity professionals as it can perform an array of cybersecurity functions, inclusive of malware scanning, analysis and penetration testing tasks. It’s the recommendation to the freshers of the cybersecurity teams due to its wide use and lesser shorter curve.

Benefits of Python in the Cybersecurity world

Design and functionalities of Python language have many benefits along with being user-friendly and easy to use for cybersecurity professionals. It is a scripting language of servers-side with an array of applications that are being used in thousands of security projects. It is a go-to language for coders in a multitude of fields such as data science, web development, mathematical computation and more importantly cyber security.

Benefits for the Developers:

Python is easy to grasp and implement:
Python was designed intentionally to be a straightforward, lightweight, easy programming language that needs minimal code to execute tasks as compared to other languages. As compared to C or Java, Python takes substantially less code for the same tasks. It provides a shorter learning curve to newbies as it is a straightforward programming language.

Debugging made easy:
Structured formatting of Python makes it easy to learn and implement. Additionally, the straightforward design and ease of task completion equivalently increase its readability. Increased and easy readability also makes debugging code extremely straightforward. This leads to easy troubleshooting from entry level coders increasing the turnaround time and efficiency.

Open-source programming language:
Similar to Linux being an open-source operating system, Python was developed as an open-source programming language. Open-source nature comes with its perks. Being an open-source programming language, it is supported, improved and upgraded all the time by a strong community of developers. Using the language is free with the advantage of flexibility and a great deal of information available in the market.

Autonomous Memory Management:
Another benefit that is easily missed out on is that Python has an auto memory management built-in by design. Pythons’ memory manager manages the memory in the language internally. This lessens the burden of the programmer or the user about the memory management concerns as it includes factors like caching, memory allocation, and segmentation.

Benefits for Cybersecurity Professionals

Speed up the process:
Due to its shorter learning curve, newbies with less technical backgrounds can give speedy results. With its ease of use, this programming language is even preferred by experienced programmers or cybersecurity professionals as the code can be executed and implemented in a jiffy.

Teams can be formed quickly:
Cybersecurity teams can give quicker turnaround times on projects even though the team consists of member who have no coding background. The complexity of the group understanding will increase with the use of a more difficult language like Java, thus decreasing the efficiency of the team. Python language offers great flexibility and benefit to the cybersecurity managers for leading teams and implementation of the projects on a faster pace.

An extensive library of Python provides the availability of the tools used for cybersecurity:
Pythons’ extensive library of modules comes in handy to cybersecurity professionals. Being open-source in nature, Pythons extensive library is constantly updated and is ready to be used by all. Cybersecurity pros won’t reinvent the tools with common tasks and in many cases can quickly search cybersecurity analysis or penetration testing tools.

Versatile use in Cybersecurity:
With the help of a deep understanding of Python and in general programming theories, cybersecurity professionals can accomplish anything using Python code. An example, Python is profoundly used for malware analysis, the sending and decoding of packets, host discovery, port scanning, accessing servers, and network scanning. Python is strongly effective at scripting, data analysis and automatic tasks resulting in it is most popular among cybersecurity professionals.

Quick Development of scripts in Python:
Simplistic code in a short period for solutions that are effective and easy to implement and debug for cybersecurity pros is a huge benefit in itself. The errors in the codes are extremely easy to detect and fix which means cybersecurity pros can solve the issues that arise quickly and reduce challenges as they go along the cybersecurity journey.

Geolocation extraction:
The real-time location of any IP address can be easily extracted using Python and Google APIs. Cybersecurity professionals can use this tool for the tracking of the place of origin of the cyber-attack which is extremely useful for many reasons. Not only can this tool have its advantages for tracking IP addresses but also can be used for blocking malicious IP addresses that would want to breach the information system.

Without using any third-party tools and only using Python as a base programming language the following can be accomplished:
Web Server Fingerprinting, Simulation of attacks, website cloning, accessing mail servers, wireless network scanning, the transmission of traffic in the network, port scanning and more are useful as cybersecurity tools.

Conclusion:
Cybersecurity has become mandatory with the increasing severity of cyber-attacks and technological advancement in recent years. Python being a resourceful language has become immensely crucial for cybersecurity professionals. It performs a multitude of functions such as malware analysis, penetrations testing and scanning functions. Securing your web applications or websites through cybersecurity is the best go-to option with the world getting a digital makeover.

Cyber professionals ensure secure and better performance of the website or web applications along with a ready to go response for any cyber-attack that might hit your digital existence. You can get in touch with us to hire dedicated developers for securing your online presence.
