item.author: matthew48550709

item.title: 如何节省时间？ ⌚ ⌚ | How to save time? ⌚
item.description:
瓦迪姆·菲利莫诺夫

发表于最大的噬时菌是社交媒体。 我看到了两种打败它的方法。

简单的：
将自己从任何地方移开。

复杂：

  • 从您的手机中删除应用程序；
  • 注销浏览器；
  • 进行两因素身份验证（使登录尝试更加困难，并且奖励将保护帐户）。
  • 阻止站点（通过扩展或 /etc/hosts）



原文:

Vadim Filimonov

Posted on Apr 25

The biggest chronophage is social media. I see two ways to defeat it.

Simple:
Remove yourself from everywhere.

Complicated:

  • Tear down apps from your phone;
  • Unlog out of browsers;
  • Put two-factor authentication (Makes login attempts harder, and a bonus will secure the account).
  • Block sites (via extensions or /etc/hosts)
item.author: vadimfilimonov

item.title: 如何在您的计算机上安装 Vite.js？ | How to Install Vite.js on your Computer?
item.description:
在忙碌的日常工作中，每个人都希望尽快完成众多工作。 并且大部分商业和个人操作都是使用应用程序进行的。

学习这项技术对于在开发领域取得进展变得至关重要。 所以，让我们快速浏览一下 Vite，然后执行一些 Vite 命令来安装它。

让我们探索安装和使用 Vite.js 的好处

热模块更换

它在 Javascript 捆绑器中可用于优化应用程序的功能。

根据官方 Vite 文档，它的性能和速度与整体可用模块分离。

裸模块解析

大多数浏览器不允许使用命令导入裸模块，例如import {createApp} from 'vue' ，因为它不是 node_modules 的相对路径。

但是，Vite 启用了 Javascript 文件中的裸导入说明符。 它重写文件内容并执行模块解析以从依赖项中找到合适的文件。

按需编译

为了让应用程序像光速一样快，Vite 只根据浏览器转发的请求编译一部分代码。 编译后，在屏幕上进行更改，所有未更改的文件都提供 304 错误代码。

此功能与传统的打包程序有很大不同，传统打包程序在更改之前编译整体代码并打包它们。

因此，开发人员更喜欢 Vite 来制作大型和复杂的应用程序。

配置选项

通过在项目根目录下配置'vite.config.js'和'vite.config.ts' ，可以优化软件，增加更多的功能。 此外，您可以使用 Koa 中间件进行自定义文件转换。

让我们通过每个步骤来安装 Vite

在进入安装过程之前，您必须确保您的系统上安装了最新版本的 Node.js。

第 1 步：检查要求并启动安装

一旦Node.js 出现并在您的计算机上运行，​​下一步就是运行“npm init @vitejs/app ”。 此外，将提供如下提供的类似列表，您必须从中选择一个项目名称。

项目名称列表：

  • 香草
  • 反应
  • 反应-ts
  • 苗条
  • 苗条的
  • 预先制定
  • 点燃元素
  • 当您从项目名称列表中选择一个选项时，它会创建一个目录，其中包含软件开发所需的基本文件。 此外，还存在“package.json”文件，其中包括“vite”作为依赖项和一些用于启动开发生态系统以启动构建的基本脚本。 我们拥有继续安装过程所需的所有知识。

    第 2 步：创建项目目录

    此外，打开系统上的终端并导航到任何目录路径以运行提供的命令： npx create-vite-app vite-test ，其中'vite-test'是项目目录的名称。

    第三步：安装命令执行

    使用“cd”命令移动到项目目录并通过执行“npm install”命令安装应用程序依赖项。

    命令执行结构：

    1. 在此之后，您将看到应用程序在 VS Code 中运行。

      第 4 步：运行开发服务器

      在这一步，我们将通过运行“npm run dev”命令启动开发服务器，成功执行后，您将能够在http://localhost:3000/上查看您的应用程序。

      此外，如果您对项目文件进行任何修改，所有更改都会立即反映在屏幕上。

      第 5 步：Vite 测试

      一旦您导航到 localhost 页面以查看您的应用程序，该屏幕将显示给您。

      现在，您已准备好将 Vite.js 用于您的软件开发项目。

      同样，对于 Windows 计算机，您需要在 macOS 上安装 Vite.js 之前安装 Noed.js。

      让我们开始在 macOS 上安装 Vite.js。 按照以下步骤操作，您就可以开始创建应用程序了。

      第 1 步：创建一个新的 Vite 应用程序

      打开终端并使用两个命令中的任何一个创建新项目。

      1. 使用 NPX： npx create-vite-app testing-vite
      2. 使用 Yarn： yarn create-vite-app testing-vite

      第 2 步：访问项目目录并安装软件包

      移动到您在第一步中创建的项目名称所在的目录。

      并安装应用程序开发所需的所有依赖项和包。 您可以使用“npm install”或“yarn”来执行此安装。

      第 3 步：运行开发服务器

      您必须在浏览器中运行服务器才能查看您的应用程序，为此，您可以使用“npm run dev”和“yarn dev”命令。

      第 4 步：Vite 测试

      通过导航到http://localhost:3000/ ，您可以测试您的服务器是否正在运行。 如果您查看类似的页面，您的服务器正在无缝运行，如下面的屏幕截图所示。

      第 5 步：创建构建

      您必须运行此命令来捆绑您的生产应用程序。 它将汇总应用程序并将输出存储在dist目录中，该目录位于软件项目的根目录中。 此外，您可以找出其中的所有静态资产。

      安装 Vite.js Vue 路由器的步骤

      它导致在浏览器的 URL 和 Vue 的元素之间建立连接，以确保在 App 上平滑呈现。

      它还有助于同步 URL，以便用户可以在您的软件解决方案中查看它。 让我们进一步了解它的安装。

      第 1 步：检查先决条件

      首先，检查您的计算机上是否有 Vue.js。 如果它存在，那么我们可以走得更远，如果它不存在，你必须在你的 vite.js 应用程序中安装它的包。

      它将确保安装最新版本的 Vue Router。

      第 2 步：创建 Javascript 文件

      您必须创建一个名为“router.js”的文件来定义路由，如下所示：

      成功执行此代码后，您必须使用定义的命令在“main.js”中注册“register.js”文件：

      第 3 步：根组件配置

      这是 Vue Router 安装的最后一步，您必须修改 App.vue 根组件以使其能够渲染所有其他应用程序组件以进行渲染。

      在此之后，您将可以为您的 Vite.js 应用程序注册自定义路由。

      单页应用的 Vite 安装

      大多数人都有一个问题，他们是否可以使用 Vite.js 创建单页应用程序。 一个可靠的答案是。 是的，您可以使用它制作 SPA 软件。

      让我们找出您必须安装和配置哪些软件包才能这样做。

      首先，我们必须执行'npm init @vitejs/app'并选择一个 Vue 模板。 我们将获得用于编译 Vue 的 Vite、Vue 和 Vite 插件。

      此外，为了处理路由，您必须安装 Vue Router，这是一个必不可少的组件，从而使您的单页应用程序功能完美无缺。 启用 Vue Router 并将其与您的应用程序链接后，它将有效地工作。

      此外，我们还可以使用 Vite Rollup 配置创建一个多页面应用程序。

      如果您发现定义路由有困难，您可以使用社区制作的插件vite-plugin-vue-router来简化任务。

      后端配置

      如果您的项目需要自定义集成，您必须按照以下提供的步骤手动配置它。 否则，您可以导航到 Awesome Vite 网站以使用传统后端提供 HTML。

      第 1 步：启用构建清单并在您​​的 Vite 配置文件中配置条目。

      第 2 步：如果模块预加载 polyfill 未禁用，则必须在条目中导入它。

      第 3 步：在 HTML 模板中添加如下所示的代码，并使用当前运行 Vite 的本地 URL 修改http://localhost:3000 。

      第 4 步：检查以下服务器配置，以确保静态资产在 Vite 环境中准确运行。 否则，应用程序不会加载图像。

      第5步：这一步侧重于生产部分。 您必须运行 vite build 命令来创建 manifest.json 文件，其内容将类似于下面提供的屏幕截图。

      记录

    2. 键应该是项目根目录的相对源路径。
    3. 键应该是非入口块的基本名称。
    4. 静态和动态信息都必须以块的形式提供。

结束

通过上述步骤，您可以毫不费力地将其安装在任何基于 Windows 或 macOS 的机器上。

如果您在任何步骤中发现任何错误或困难，您应该尝试验证先决条件，使用提供的步骤确认配置，然后继续。

您可以将 Vite 与任何 Vue 应用程序一起使用； 无论是 Vue 2 还是 Vue 3，它都兼容它们。

敬请关注更多精彩文章和指南。



原文:

In the hustle of day-to-day work, everyone wants to complete their numerous jobs as fast as possible. And most of the business and personal operations are conducted using applications.

Vite.js has made its debut in the market for making apps faster, which has gained a lot of popularity and endorsement from industry experts.

Vite Javascript is a no bundler modern technology development environment, allowing applications to render more precisely and efficiently through its native ES module importing and HMR.

It has become essential to learn this tech for moving forward in the development domain. So, let’s have a quick overview of Vite and then execute some Vite commands for installing it.

What does Vite.js Offer?

Let’s explore the perks of installing and using Vite.js

Hot Module Replacement

HMR is the most popular feature of Vite.js as it allows to update the content on the web page immediately without needing a browser refresh. It is available in the Javascript bundler to optimize the functionality of your application.

As per the official Vite documentation, its performance and speed are decoupled from the overall available modules.

Bare Module Resolving

Most of the browsers don’t allow importing of bare modules using commands, such as import {createApp} from ‘vue’, as it is not a relative path to the node_modules.

However, Vite enables the bare import specifiers from the Javascript files. It rewrites the file content and executes module resolution for locating the suitable file from the dependencies.

On-demand compilation

For making the application fast as light-speed, Vite compiles only a portion of code according to the request forwarded by the browser. After compilation, changes are made on-screen, and all unaltered files provide a 304 error code.

This feature is quite different from the traditional bundlers, which compile the overall code and bundle them before changing.

Due to this, developers prefer Vite for making large and complex applications.

Configuration Option

Vite offers a wide array of modules to extend the functionality of your application.

By configuring ‘vite.config.js’ and ‘vite.config.ts’ in the project’s root directory, you can optimize the software to add more features to it. In addition, you can use Koa middleware for custom file transformation.

Let’s Get through Each Step to Install Vite

Before moving to the installation procedure, you must ensure that the latest version of Node.js is installed on your system.

Step 1: Checking Requirements and Initiating installation

Once the Node.js is present and running on your computer, the next step is to run the ‘npm init @vitejs/app.’ Further, a similar list as below provided will be available, and you have to select a project name from it.

Project Name List:

  • vanilla
  • vue
  • vue-ts
  • react
  • react-ts
  • svelte
  • svelte-ts
  • preact
  • preact-ts
  • lit-element
  • lit-element-ts In this installation, we have selected vanilla as the project name.

When you select an option from the project name list, it creates a directory containing essential files required for software development. ‘index.html’, ‘main.js’, ‘style.css’, ‘favicon.svg’ and some additional files for performing npm and Git operations.

Moreover, the ‘package.json’ file is also present, which includes ‘vite’ as a dependency and some basic scripts to initiate the dev ecosystem for starting the build. We have all the required knowledge to move on with the installation process.

Step 2: Creating Project Directory

Further, open the terminal on your system and navigate to any directory path to run the command as provided: npx create-vite-app vite-test, where ‘vite-test’ is the name of the project directory.

Step 3: Installation command execution

Move to the project directory using the ‘cd’ command and install the application dependency by executing the ‘npm install’ command.

Command execution structure:

  1. cd vite-project
  2. npm install

After this, you will see the application running in the VS Code.

Step 4: Running the Dev Server

At this step, we will start the dev server by running the ‘npm run dev’ command, and after its successful execution, you will be able to view your application on http://localhost:3000/.

Also, if you perform any modification in the project files, all the changes will be immediately reflected on the screen.

Step 5: Vite Test

This screen will be displayed to you once you navigate to the localhost page to view your application.

Now, you are ready to use Vite.js for your software development projects.

Vite Install on macOS: Step-by-Step Guide

Likewise, for Windows computers, you need to install Noed.js before installing Vite.js on macOS.

Let’s get started with installing Vite.js on macOS. Follow the below steps, and you are good to go with your application creation.

Step 1: Creating a new Vite App

Open the terminal and create new projects using any of the two commands.

  1. Using NPX: npx create-vite-app testing-vite
  2. Using Yarn: yarn create-vite-app testing-vite

‘testing-vite’ is the project’s name, and you can replace it with any other term of your choice.\

Step 2: Accessing Project Directory and Installing Packages

Move to the directory with the project name you have created in the first step.

And install all the dependencies and packages required to work with the application development. You can use ‘npm install’ or ‘yarn’ to perform this installation.

Step 3: Running the Dev Server

You have to run the server in the browser to view your application, and for this, you can use ‘npm run dev’ and ‘yarn dev’ commands.

Step 4: Vite Test

By navigating to http://localhost:3000/, you can test whether your server is running or not. Your server is running seamlessly if you view a similar page, as shown in the screengrab below.

Step 5: Creating the build

You have to run this command for bundling up your production application. It will Rollup the App and store the output in the dist directory, present in the root of your software project. In addition, you can find out all the static assets in it.

Steps to Install Vite.js Vue Router

Vue Router is an essential component of every application. It leads to establishing a connection between the browser’s URL and elements of Vue to ensure smooth rendering over the App.

It also aids in syncing URLs so that the user can view that in your software solution. Let’s move further to understand its installation.

Step 1: Checking Prerequisites

First, check whether you have Vue.js on your computer or not. If it is present, then we are good to go further, and if it’s not, you must install its packages in your vite.js application.

‘npm I –save vue-router@v4.0.0-alpha.11’ is the command you have to execute to install Vue Router packages in your Vite App.

It will ensure that the latest version of the Vue Router is installed.

Step 2: Creating a Javascript file

You have to create a file with the name ‘router.js’ for defining the routes as displayed below:

After successful execution of this code, you have to register the ‘register.js’ file in the ‘main.js’ using the command as defined:

Step 3: Root Component Configuration

This is the last step of Vue Router installation, in which you have to modify the App.vue root component to enable it to render all other app components for rendering.

After this, you will have the leverage to register custom routes for your Vite.js application.

Vite Install for Single Page Application

Most people have a question, about whether they can create a single page application with Vite.js or not. A reliable answer is. Yes, you can fabricate SPA software with it.

Let’s find out which packages you have to install and configure for doing it so.

At first, we have to execute ‘npm init @vitejs/app’ and pick out a Vue template. We will get a Vite, Vue, and Vite plugin for compiling the Vue.

In addition, for handling the routes, you have to install Vue Router, which is an essential component, leading to flawless functionality of your single-page application. It will work efficiently after enabling Vue Router and linking it with your App.

Vite has a significant role here, as most operations are Vue-based. Moreover, we can also create a multi-page application using the Vite Rollup configuration.

If you find difficulty defining the routes, you can use a community-made plugin vite-plugin-vue-router to simplify the task.

Backend Configuration

If your project requires custom integration, you must follow the below-provided steps to configure it manually. Otherwise, you can navigate to the Awesome Vite website to serve the HTML with a traditional backend.

Step 1: Enable the build manifest and configure the entry in your Vite config file.

Step 2: If module preload polyfill is not disabled, you have to import it in your entry.

Step 3: Add the below-shown code in your HTML template and modify the http://localhost:3000 with the local URL, where Vite is currently running.

Step 4: Check the following server configuration to assure that static asset precisely work in the Vite environment. Otherwise, the application won’t load images.

Step 5: This step focuses on the production part. You have to run a vite build command to create a manifest.json file, whose content will look like the screenshot provided below.

The JSON file must contain:

  • Record
  • The key should be the relative source path from the project root.
  • Key should be the base name for non-entry chunks.
  • Both static and dynamic information must be available in chunks.

Concluding Up

Vite is a robust technology, and one should always know to install it on their system. And you can effortlessly install it on any Windows or macOS-based machine with the above-given step.

If you find any error or difficulty at any step, you should try to verify the prerequisites, confirm the configuration with the provided steps, and then continue it.

You can use Vite with any Vue application; whether it’s Vue 2 or Vue 3, it is compatible with both of them.

Stay Tuned for more exciting articles and guides.

item.title: Curso Node.JS 基础 🧰 | Curso Node.JS Essencial 🧰
item.description:
节点.JS

耸人听闻，nãoé？！

在 JavaScript 或 trabalhar com um código pelo Node.JS devido ao processamento do motor V8 do Chrome 上的所有可视化页面。

开源： é uma plataforma de código aberto, isso significa que você pode ter acesso ao código fonte do Node.JS e realizar suas próprias customizações ou mesmo contribuir para a comunidade de forma direta;

  • Escalável: Node.JS foi criado para construir aplicações web escaláveis, como podemos ver na sua documentação oficial . Escalável： Node.JS foi criado para construir aplicações web escaláveis，como podemos ver na sua documentação oficial 。

    • O Node.JS ainda apresenta uma curva de aprendizado relativamente menor comparado à outras linguagens voltadas ao Back-End e possui uma comunidade gigante disposta a ajudar. O Node.JS ainda apresenta uma curva de aprendizado relativamente menor comparado à outras linguagens voltadas ao Back-End e possui uma comunidade gigante disposta a ajudar。 E sem dúvida a união dessas forças é o super poder do Node.JS! E sem dúvida a união dessas forçasé o super poder do Node.JS！

    Agora que você já sabe tudo sobre Node.JS, vamos começar uma jornada rumo ao conhecimento e um curso com o essencial sobre esta tecnologia incrível. Agora que você já sabe tudo sobre Node.JS, vamos começar uma jornada rumo ao conhecimento e um curso com o essencial sobre esta tecnologia incrível。



    原文:

    Node.JS é uma ferramenta amplamente utilizada no universo de desenvolvimento e todos que atuam na área de tecnologia já ouviram falar, nem que seja nos milhares de anúncios de cursos ou mesmo de vagas. Mas você se pergunta: afinal, o que é Node.js?

    Node.JS e JavaScript

    JavaScript é uma linguagem de programação que originalmente foi desenvolvida para trazer maior interatividade aos websites através da manipulação do DOM (Document Object Model). Vamos conferir a definição do livro que é uma referência na temática, o Eloquent JavaScript:

    O JavaScript foi introduzido em 1995 como uma forma de adicionar dinamicidade à páginas da web no navegador Netscape Navigator. Desde então, a linguagem foi adotada por todos os outros principais navegadores gráficos da web. Ela tornou possíveis o desenvolvimento das aplicações modernas da web - aplicações com os quais você pode interagir diretamente sem recarregar a página a cada ação. JavaScript também é usado em sites mais tradicionais para fornecer várias formas de interatividade de forma mais inteligente.

    Portanto, de forma resumida, podemos entender que o JavaScript foi pensado para ser rápido, dinâmico e acessível. A linguagem interpretada possibilita subir ou trabalhar em suas aplicações sem precisar configurar todo um ambiente complexo.

    Essas características motivaram o engenheiro de software Ryan Dahl a desenvolver um ambiente, como um programa que você instala no seu computador, que trabalha com a linguagem JavaScript fora do navegador e pelo lado do servidor, via terminal, de uma maneira menos restritiva. Para quem já trabalhou com servidores IIS ou servidores PHP, este assunto não é complexo. Mas, para quem é iniciante muitos paradigmas aqui podem ser bem complexos.

    Recomendo então que estude o básico do Javascript no mínimo para poder seguir esta trilha. É esperado que você tenha conhecimentos mais sólidos em programação Javascript (pelo menos o básico) e é desejável também, conhecimento em HTML e CSS. Um total iniciante não tendo nutrido seu conhecimento da base da web e seus protocolos ficaria deslocado e não teria um bom proveito.

    Então, vamos lá!

    Node.JS

    O JavaScript nasceu para atender demandas voltadas ao Front e como as necessidades aumentam de acordo com o crescimento tecnológico, surgiu a ideia de utilizar uma mesma linguagem no lado do cliente e do servidor para otimizar processos e serviços. Dessa forma, o Node.JS aparece como uma alternativa viável para programação Back-End por se tratar de um ambiente para desenvolvimento utilizando a linguagem JavaScript.

    De acordo com sua definição oficial, o Node é um runtime, que nada mais é do que um conjunto de códigos, API’s, ou seja, são bibliotecas responsáveis pelo tempo de execução (é o que faz o seu programa rodar) que funciona como um interpretador de JavaScript fora do ambiente do navegador web.

    É importante frisar que o Node.JS é um ambiente de execução assíncrono, isto é, ele trabalha de modo a não bloquear no momento da execução da aplicação, delegando os processos demorados a um segundo plano.

    Ele dá muito certo com os servidores de arquitetura “single threaded”, isto significa que todos os pedidos para o servidor são executados no mesmo tópico - em vez de serem gerados em processos separados. Um dos grandes diferenciais da parceria Node.JS e Javascript é o bom desempenho no uso de APIs, já que o Javascript faz bastante uso de APIs assíncronas.

    Você deve estar se perguntando: “Beleza, já entendi o que é o Node.JS e seu diferencial quando o assunto é o seu uso com o Javascript, mas o que isso significa?” Vamos entender um pouco mais... vem comigo, que te explico...

    Como o Node.JS funciona?

    O Node é capaz de interpretar um código JavaScript, igual ao que o navegador faz. Sendo assim, quando o navegador recebe um comando em JavaScript, ele o interpreta e depois executa as instruções fornecidas.

    O Node é fantástico porque torna possível o envio de instruções (os nossos códigos) sem precisar de um navegador ativo, basta ter o Node.JS instalado e utilizar o terminal para executar um programa construído em JavaScript. Algo bem parecido com o que o pessoal que programa em Python também tem ao usar sua IDLE, mas, neste caso estamos falando de Javascript.

    Além disso, você pode utilizar apenas uma linguagem de programação para tratar requisições entre cliente e servidor.
    Sensacional, não é?!

    Para que todo esse processo seja possível de ocorrer fora do navegador, o Node utiliza uma outra ferramenta chamada de Chrome's V8 JavaScript Engine. É esse motor V8 do Chrome que compila e executa o código JavaScript no lugar de apenas interpretá-lo.

    Ainda parece muito confuso? Vem comigo que te explico melhor…

    O motor V8 da Google é o centro, o coração que processa todo o código JavaScript do navegador para que sua máquina compreenda e disponibilize os recursos e interações. Só é possível visualizar páginas em JavaScript ou trabalhar com um código pelo Node.JS devido ao processamento do motor V8 do Chrome.

    Características do Node.JS

    Node.JS pode ser utilizado nas famosas APIS Rest, web scrapping, chatbots, IoT, web servers, aplicações Desktop, tudo devido a sua característica altamente versátil. Dentre as principais, podemos citar:

    • Multiplataforma: permite criar desde aplicativos desktop, aplicativos móveis e até sites SaaS;

    • Multi-paradigma: é possível programar em diferentes paradigmas, como: Orientado a Objetos, funcional, imperativo e dirigido à eventos;

    • Open Source: é uma plataforma de código aberto, isso significa que você pode ter acesso ao código fonte do Node.JS e realizar suas próprias customizações ou mesmo contribuir para a comunidade de forma direta;

    • Escalável: Node.JS foi criado para construir aplicações web escaláveis, como podemos ver na sua documentação oficial.

    O Node.JS ainda apresenta uma curva de aprendizado relativamente menor comparado à outras linguagens voltadas ao Back-End e possui uma comunidade gigante disposta a ajudar. E sem dúvida a união dessas forças é o super poder do Node.JS!

    Agora que você já sabe tudo sobre Node.JS, vamos começar uma jornada rumo ao conhecimento e um curso com o essencial sobre esta tecnologia incrível.

item.title: 在 Javascript 中蚀刻草图 | Etch A Sketch in Javascript
item.description:
开始一个新的编程项目有一些令人兴奋的事情！ 我喜欢这种新鲜的感觉，上一个项目的所有问题（理论上）都已解决，我们开始了新的旅程，以创造一些尚未引起丑陋头脑的激进新问题。 我的下一个项目将不同于我迄今为止所做的任何事情。 这是来自 The Odin Project ( Etch A Sketch ) 的 Etch A Sketch 项目。

概述：

根据项目指南的要求是：

  1. 使用 JavaScript 创建方形 div 的网格（不直接编码到 HTML 中）。 只要不直接编码，就可以使用我选择的任何方法创建网格。

  2. 设置悬停效果，当鼠标经过 div 时更改网格背景颜色。 我打算稍微偏离这条指令，并使用点击和拖动技术，因为我觉得它可以更好地控制画布上的“绘画”。

  3. 添加一个按钮，该按钮将清除网格并询问用户用于新网格的正方形数量。 同样，我会在这里做一些不同的事情。 在尝试其他严格遵守说明的版本时，弹出窗口导致用户在单击弹出框上的“确定”后立即开始为网格着色，我发现这违反直觉。

  4. 可选：使用一个随机按钮，该按钮可以绘制出彩虹效果的一系列颜色。 然后有另一个按钮，将每个通道绘制为 10% 的黑色，以便在 10 个通道后网格完全黑色。

在写这篇文章时仔细阅读这些说明，我意识到我没有使用“RGB”彩虹按钮，但我确实添加了其他选项来扩展这个项目，只是为了给它我自己的天赋。 这是我上周完成的 GUI 预览，然后我花了三天的周末与家人共度时光：

如您所见，我添加了“画笔颜色”按钮、“背景颜色”按钮、“变亮”按钮、“网格大小”范围滑块输入和网格切换复选框。 除了上述可选和必需元素之外，还添加了这些元素。

对于网格大小滑块，当您增加网格大小时，单元格的大小将变小，因为画布将是一个恒定大小。 到目前为止我还没有看到的一件事是背景颜色更改选项。 大多数人都有画布常见的白色背景，但有人可能想要粉红色背景并在上面涂上蓝色，所以我觉得我的应该提供其他有用的选项并迫使我跳出框框思考.

我的一个同行对该项目的演绎实现了一个网格切换按钮，它提供了我喜欢的画布效果。 我认为你可以使用网格来排列东西很酷，然后在需要预览时将其关闭。 所以，当我看到它时，我决定在我自己的项目中实现同样的东西。

完成 GUI 后，除了添加彩虹色选项之类的小改动外，我将在本周完成项目的其余部分。 完成后，我将添加一篇新的博客文章，总结该项目以及我面临的道路上的颠簸等。上周我确实花了半天时间在网格上工作，我有点难过，但我确实想出了在上周末与家人共度时光时可以尝试一些事情，我期待着尝试这些并取得一些进展！ 直到下一个......要好！

照片由@felipepelaquim在Unsplash上拍摄



原文:

There is something exciting about starting a new programming project! I love that it feels fresh, all the problems from the last project are (theoretically) solved and here we are, embarking on a new journey to create some radical new problems that have yet to rear their ugly heads. My next project will be different from anything I've done to date. It's the Etch A Sketch project from The Odin Project (Etch A Sketch).

Overview:

Etch A Sketch is a "doodling" app of sorts that will allow the user to draw on a grid of square <div>'s. The requirements per the project guidelines are to:

  1. Use JavaScript to create a grid of square div's (not directly coded into the HTML). The grid can be created using any method of my choosing so long as it's not coded directly.

  2. Set up a hover effect that changes the grid background colors when the mouse passes over the divs. I plan to deviate from this instruction slightly and will use a click-and-drag technique as I feel it offers more control over the "painting" on the canvas.

  3. Add a button that will clear the grid and ask the user for the number of squares to use for a new grid. Again, I will do something different here. In trying out others' version of this where they strictly adhered to the instructions, the popup caused the user to immediately start coloring the grids in after clicking "ok" on the popup box which I found counter intuitive.

  4. Optional: Use a random button that paints in a range of colors that gives a rainbow effect. Then have another button that paints each pass at 10% of black so that after 10 passes the grid is completely black.

In going over these instructions while writing this, I realized that I left off the "RGB" rainbow button, but I did add other options to expand on this project just a little bit to give it my own flair. This is a sneak peek of my GUI that I finished last week before I took a three day weekend to spend time with family:

As you can see, I added a "brush color" button, a "background color" button, a "lighten" button, and a "grid size" range slider input, and a grid toggle checkbox. These were added in addition to the optional and required elements above.

For the grid size slider, as you increase the size of the grid, the size of the cells will get smaller as the canvas will be a constant size. One of the things I haven't seen thus far is a background color changing option. Most everyone has had a white background which is common for a canvas, but someone may want to have a pink background and paint on it with blue, so I felt like mine should provide other options that were useful and forced me to think outside the box.

One of my peers' renditions of this project implemented a grid toggle button that gave the canvas effect which I loved. I thought it was cool that you could use the grid to line things up, then turn it off if you needed it for a preview. So, I decided then, when I saw it, that I was going to implement the same thing in my own project.

Having the GUI done, aside from minor changes such as adding the rainbow color option, I will shoot to have the rest of the project completed this week. Once done, I will add a new blog post that will summarize the project and what bumps in the road I faced etc. I did spend half a day last week working on the grid and I was a bit stumped, but I did come up with some things to try while spending time with family this past weekend and I look forward to trying those out and making some progress! Until the next one...be good!

Photo by @felipepelaquim on Unsplash
item.author: warmastr

item.title: 什么是区块链开发 - 初学者的区块链定义 | What is Blockchain Development - Blockchain Definition for Beginners
item.description:
现在，如果你是社交媒体的忠实拥护者，准确地说是 Twitter，你可能遇到过区块链、web3、加密货币等术语……能够解释它们的意思的人并不多，或者甚至它们的全部内容。 我处于相同的位置，并将尝试以最简单的方式分享我能够学到的东西。

我们都遇到过人们如何通过加密货币赚取数百万美元的故事，对吧？ 我们还听说过加密货币正在接管的说法，这意味着在不久的将来将不需要实体银行，这对于一切的发展方向来说可能是正确的。 为了让您保持循环，让我们了解这一切。

在本指南中，我们将讨论区块链开发以及在该技术上运行的一些应用程序。

让我们开始吧：

什么是区块链开发

用简单的英语来说，区块链是一个同时分布在多台计算机上的数据库。

从技术上讲，区块链是一种分散的、不断发展的数字记录公共分类账。 我们所说的去中心化是指它是一个相互关联的系统，其中没有一个实体是权威。

区块链技术中去中心化系统的一个很好的例子包括比特币和以太坊。

在过去的几年里，区块链技术获得了普及，因为它带来了网络之间的信任，并且还提供了系统之间真正的隐私连接。

随着科技行业的不断发展，这意味着它将继续为不同领域的开发人员在区块链领域开辟更多机会。 它的增长已经在金融、房地产、医疗保健等不同领域形成......

区块链的历史

作为一名成长中的开发人员，我一直着迷于了解在特定技术出现之前发生的事件，而区块链不会有任何不同。

作为劳拉·琳妮，
历史是一种资源。

这一切都始于分布式账本的概念，该概念可以追溯到 90 年代，当时 Stuart Haber 和 Scott Stometta 想要实现一个无法篡改文档时间戳的系统。 在第三人 Dave 的帮助下，他们能够将 Merkle 树合并到设计中，通过允许将多个文档证书收集到一个块中来提高其效率。

首先到 2009 年，当时第一个去中心化区块链由中本聪概念化，他构建了比特币，正式将区块链引入世界。

几年后，该技术被广泛接受，并为在在线钱包等应用程序上部署加密货币开辟了道路。 这使其也可以用于数字支付系统。

通过这种流行，金融市场和应用程序开始使用区块链，而不是现金交易。 这使人们无需银行即可在全球范围内进行交易。

区块链技术已经形成了三个主要阶段，即：

  • 区块链 1.0 - 比特币开发
    这是比特币诞生的时候，这导致更多的组织加入了数字革命，因此采用了该技术

  • 区块链 2.0 - 以太坊开发
    它的开发是为了利用区块链技术的全部功能。 它的开发人员 Vitalik Buterin 开始研究他认为除了作为点对点网络之外还可以执行各种功能的区块链。

它于 2015 年正式推出，使其成为区块链技术的最大应用之一。 这是由于其支持智能合约的能力。

  • 区块链 3.0 - 未来 公平地说，区块链并不止于比特币和以太坊。 有几个新项目试图利用整个系统。

随着大型科技公司的参与，我们希望看到更多充分利用区块链发展的发展。

区块链如何运作

可以解释区块链如何运作的工作流程的一个很好的例子是银行场景。 在银行系统中看到区块链全面影响的主要系统之一。

让我们通过一个例子来分解它，假设我们有两个人试图进行交易。 最初，为了将钱汇给肯尼亚的 B 人，这将如何运作：

在此过程之后，B 人将不得不等待两到三天，交易才会反映在他们的银行中。

这整个过程会有一些与之相关的问题：

  • 该过程成本高昂，因为发送方和接收方都会产生费用。
  • 帐户黑客攻击黑客可能会入侵银行，从而破坏和损失资金。
  • 金融危机也可能发生。

如果通过区块链技术进行相同的交易，流程将如何：

所需要的只是接收者的比特币钱包来启动交易，并且在几秒钟内，它将反映接收者在肯尼亚的钱包。

区块链在银行系统中所做的是试图通过引入以下方法来解决旧银行方法的问题：

  • 一个去中心化的系统。
  • 分布式账本
  • 激励验证
  • 共识算法
  • 密码算法。

区块链的类型

区块链是最近兴起的技术之一，我们已经让不同的公司尝试制定区块链解决方案来满足他们的供应链需求，这一法案将不得不呼吁哪种类型的区块链最适合对于他们试图解决的项目。

话虽如此，区块链解决方案将不得不依赖于定义区块链类型的两个主要特征，即：

  • 无许可区块链
  • 许可的区块链

正是在这两个主要类别下，四种主要类型的区块链网络位于：

  1. 公共区块链它属于无许可类别，解释这个概念的一个很好的例子是比特币和以太坊网络。 公共区块链主要用于交换和挖掘加密货币。

  • 私有区块链

    它也被称为托管区块链，属于许可区块链类别，由单个组织控制。 私有区块链的一些常见示例包括： 企业对企业虚拟货币交换网络 Ripple 和 Hyperledger，这是一个开源区块链应用程序的伞形项目。

  • 联盟区块链它属于许可区块链类别，它通常由一组组织管理，这意味着与私有区块链相比，它们可以享受更多的去中心化。

    它的一个应用程序可以与供应链领域相关联，货讯通等公司在该领域开发了全球航运商业网络联盟，这是一个非营利性区块链联盟，旨在实现航运业数字化并允许海运。

  • 混合区块链

    它们由单个组织控制，但也处理未经许可的流程。 解释这一概念的一个很好的例子是 IBM Food Trust，它旨在提高整个食品供应链的效率。 在此处阅读更多相关信息。

    • 区块链的应用

    当我们想到区块链时，我们的思想应该超越加密货币、NFT 和比特币。 尽管它能够创造更高的透明度和公平性，但它也帮助企业节省了时间和金钱。

    我们已经看到了该技术在各个部门和部门的影响，这意味着随着我们的前进，它的影响正在得到认可。

    实施区块链的一些常见应用包括：

    • 音乐版税追踪
    • 跨境支付
    • 个人身份安全
    • 反洗钱追踪系统
    • 供应链和物流监控
    • 投票机制
    • 广告洞察
    • 原创内容创作
    • 加密货币交易所
    • 房地产加工平台

    到此为止，您已经了解了区块链开发的全部内容，也许您可​​能想知道我如何才能认识到自己是区块链开发人员？ 我可能需要哪些技能？ 好吧，现在让我们谈谈这个，但首先，让我们了解谁是区块链开发人员？

    谁是区块链开发者

    区块链开发人员是负责开发和增强与区块链相关的应用程序的人，这些应用程序通常称为 dApp、智能联系人、架构和协议。

    我们在此类别下有两种主要类型的开发人员，即：

    • 核心区块链开发人员

      他们设计运行区块链解决方案所需的协议。 他们使用低级编程语言。

    • 软件/应用程序开发人员

      他们实施核心开发人员的设计以创建一个现实的区块链。 这包括实施智能合约和开发去中心化应用程序。

    成为区块链开发人员所需的技能

    很明显，为了获得区块链开发人员的头衔，您需要各种技能。 一些最重要的包括：

    • 了解区块链架构。
    • 具备数据结构和编码方面的知识
    • 了解如何构建和开发智能合约
    • 密码学基础
    • 区块链开发人员的职责

      根据您的开发人员类型，您的职责会有所不同。

      核心区块链开发人员的角色

      1. 他们了解并实施区块链的特性和功能。
      2. 设计区块链协议。
      3. 他们确保区块链网络顺利运行。

      软件/应用程序区块链开发人员的角色

      1. 他们设计和实施智能合约
      2. 为 Dapps 开发和部署交互式前端设计。
      3. 处理与其他应用程序和服务的集成。

      成为区块链开发人员的优势

      您将在该领域获得的主要优势之一是高水平的工作需求。 作为区块链开发人员，这是您大放异彩的时候，因为机会正在蓬勃发展。 不仅如此，我们在当今时代给区块链开发者提供了无穷无尽的机会，而伴随着职称的是一份可观的薪水。

      核心区块链开发人员平均每年可赚取约 150,000 美元，而区块链软件开发人员每年可赚取 100,000 美元。

      如何开始区块链开发

      为了了解我们在这里讨论的所有内容，您可能需要一些指导。 幸运的是，您不必自己搜索资源，因为我为您完成了这些。 您只需要选择一个并开始学习。

      在线课程

      包起来

      所以，伙计们，这就是关于区块链开发以及如何成为区块链开发者的全部知识。 现在继续并掌握上述技能以进入区块链开发。

      如果你已经读到这里，我真的很感激。

      让我们社交😊 Twitter | 优酷| 领英|

      享受编码❤

      Jagad Yudha Awali - Jagad Yudha Awali - Apr 20 4月20日



    原文:

    Now, if you are a big fan of social media, Twitter to be precise chances are that you have come across terms like blockchain, web3, cryptocurrency, etc... It not a lot of people who are able to explain what they mean or even what they are all about. I was in the same position and will try to share what I was able to learn in the simplest way possible.

    We have all come across stories of how people have made millions of dollars through cryptocurrency, right? We have also heard claims that cryptocurrencies are taking over meaning there won't be a need to have physical banks in the near future, well that might be true with the direction everything is headed. To keep you in the loop, let's understand all this.

    In this guide, we are going to talk about Blockchain development and some of the applications running on this technology.

    Let's get started:

    What is Blockchain Development

    In simple English, a blockchain is a database that is distributed across multiple computers at the same time.

    In technical terms, a blockchain is a decentralized consistently evolving public ledger of digital records. By Decentralized we mean it is a system of interconnected systems where no single entity is the authority.

    A good example of a decentralized system in Blockchain technology includes both Bitcoin and Ethereum.

    In the past few years, Blockchain technology has gained popularity because it brings trust among networks and also offers genuine privacy connections among systems.

    As the tech industry continues to evolve, this means it will continue to open up opportunities for developers in different domains more so in the blockchain field. Its growth has been seen taking shape in different fields like finance, real estate, health care, etc...

    History of Blockchain

    As a growing developer, I have always been fascinated to learn about the events that came before a particular technology came into existence, and that won't be any different with Blockchain.

    As Laura Linney,
    History is a resource.

    It all began with the concept of distributed ledger which dates back to the 90s when Stuart Haber and Scott Stometta wanted to implement a system where document timestamps could not be tampered with. With the help of a third person Dave, they were able to incorporate Merkle trees into the design, which improved its efficiency by allowing several document certificates to be collected into one block.

    First forward to 2009, when the first decentralized blockchain was conceptualized by Satoshi Nakamoto, by his building Bitcoin which officially introduced Blockchain to the world.

    A few years later, the technology was widely accepted and it opened ways for cryptocurrency deployment on applications like online wallets. This allowed it to be used in digital payments systems too.

    Through this popularity, financial markets & applications started using Blockchain beyond cash transactions. This allowed people to conduct transactions globally without needing banks.

    Blockchain technology has taken shape across three major phases, that is:

    • Blockchain 1.0 - Bitcoin Development
      This is when Bitcoin came into existence, this led to more organizations joining the digital revolution hence adopting the technology

    • Blockchain 2.0 - Ethereum Development
      It was developed to leverage the full capabilities of blockchain technology. Its developer Vitalik Buterin started working on what he felt would be a blockchain that can perform various functions in addition to being a peer-to-peer network.

    It was officially launched in 2015 making it one of the biggest applications of blockchain technology. This is due to its capability to support smart contracts.

    • Blockchain 3.0 - The future It will be fair to say that, Blockchain does not stop with Bitcoin and Ethereum. There have been a couple of new projects trying to leverage the whole system.

    With the involvement of big tech companies, we hope to see more development taking full advantage of blockchain development.

    How Blockchain Works

    A good example that can explain the workflow of how blockchain operates is a bank scenario. One of the major systems that have seen the full effect of blockchain in the banking system.

    Let's break it down with the help of an example, take a scenario where we have two people trying to conduct a transaction. Person A is based in Canada and person B is based in Kenya. Initially how this will work in order for the money to be sent to person B in Kenya is:

    Person A would need to physically go to the bank, where he will then initiate the transaction via the bank teller, instructing him/her to send a certain amount of money to person B's bank details. After this process person B will have to wait two to three days before the transaction reflects in their bank.

    This whole process will have some issues associated with it:

    • The process is costly because both the sender and receiver will incur charges.
    • Account hacking Hackers might hack banks hence disrupting and loss of funds.
    • Financial crisis may also occur.

    What if the same transaction was to happen over blockchain technology, how would the process be:

    Person A wouldn't have to leave home since all the transactions can be done from anywhere on the smartphone or the computer. All that would be needed is a bitcoin wallet of the receiver to initiate the transaction and in a matter of a few seconds, it will reflect the receiver's wallet in Kenya.

    What blockchain is doing in the banking system, is trying to resolve issues of the old banking methods by introducing:

    • A decentralized system.
    • Distributed ledger
    • Incentives validation
    • Consensus algorithm
    • Cryptography algorithm.

    Types of Blockchain

    Blockchain is one of the technologies that have been on the rise in the recent past, we have had different companies try to formulate a blockchain solution to fill their supply chain needs, this act will have to call as to what type of blockchain is best suited for the project they are trying to solve.

    That being said, the blockchain solution will have to rely upon the two main characteristics defining the blockchain types, that is:

    • Permissionless blockchains
    • Permissioned blockchains

    It's under these two main categories that four main types of blockchain networks lie within:

    1. Public blockchains It falls under the permissionless category and a good example to explain this concept is the Bitcoin and Ethereum network. Public blockchains are primarily used for exchanging and mining cryptocurrency.

    • Private blockchains

      It is also referred to as managed blockchains, it falls under the permissioned blockchains category where it is controlled by a single organization. Some common examples of private blockchains include; business-to-business virtual currency exchange network Ripple and Hyperledger, which is an umbrella project of open-source blockchain applications.

  • Consortium blockchains It falls under the permissioned blockchains category, it is usually governed by a group of organizations meaning they get to enjoy more decentralization compared to private blockchains.

    One of its applications can be linked to the supply chain sector where companies like CargoSmart have developed the Global Shipping Business Network Consortium, which is a not-for-profit blockchain consortium that aims to digitalize the shipping industry and allow maritime.

  • Hybrid blockchains

    They are controlled by a single organization but also deal with permissionless processes. A good example explaining this concept is the IBM Food Trust, which was developed to improve efficiency throughout the whole food supply chain. Read more about it here.

    • Applications of Blockchain

    When we think of Blockchain, our minds should go beyond cryptocurrency, NFTs, and bitcoin. Despite its ability to create more transparency and fairness, it has also helped businesses save time and money.

    We have seen the impact of the technology in various departments and sectors meaning its effect is being recognized as we move on.

    Some of the common applications where blockchain is implemented include:

    • NFT marketplaces
    • Music royalties tracking
    • Cross-border payments
    • Personal identity security
    • Anti-money laundering tracking system
    • Supply chain and logistics monitoring
    • Voting mechanism
    • Advertising insights
    • Original content creation
    • Cryptocurrency exchange
    • Real estate processing platform

    Now by this point, you have an understanding of what blockchain development is all about, perhaps you might wonder how do I get to recognize myself as a blockchain developer? What are some of the skills that I might require? well let's talk about that now, but first, let's understand Who is a Blockchain Developer?

    Who is a Blockchain Developer

    A Blockchain Developer is a person who is responsible for developing and enhancing Blockchain-related applications which are commonly known as dApps, Smart Contacts, architecture, and protocols.

    We have two main types of developers under this category, that is:

    • Core blockchain developers

      They design protocols required to run a blockchain solution. They use Low-level programming languages.

    • Software/App developers

      They implement the designs by the core developers to create a realistic blockchain. That includes implementing smart contracts and developing decentralized applications.

    Skills Needed to Be a Blockchain Developer

    It is obvious that in order to get the title of a Blockchain developer you will need a variety of skills. Some of the most essential include:

    • Have an understanding of Blockchain Architecture.
    • Have the knowledge of Data Structures and Coding
    • Learn how to build and develop smart contracts
    • Basics of Cryptography
    • Web development

    Responsibilities of a Blockchain Developer

    Depending on the type of developer you are, your responsibilities will vary from the other.

    Roles of a Core Blockchain Developer

    1. They understand and implement the features and functionalities of the blockchain.
    2. Design blockchain protocols.
    3. They make sure the blockchain network functions smoothly.

    Roles of a Software/App Blockchain Developer

    1. They design and implement Smart Contracts
    2. Development and deployment of interactive front-end designs for Dapps.
    3. Deal with integration with other applications and services.

    Advantages of being a Blockchain Developer

    One of the major advantages that you will get in this field is the high level of job demand. As a blockchain developer, this is your time to shine because opportunities are thriving. Many fortune 500 companies like IBM, Accenture, Openledger, and Capgemini are on the hunt for eligible blockchain developers.

    That's not all there is, we have endless opportunities for blockchain developers in the current age, and accompanying the job title is a handsome salary.

    A core blockchain developer on average would make around $150,000 annually while blockchain software developers make $100,000 per annum.

    How to Get Started with Blockchain Development

    In order to learn all that we have discussed here, you'll probably want some guidance. Luckily, you won't have to search for the resources yourself because I did that for you. All you need is to pick one and get started learning.

    Online Courses

    YouTube Courser

    Wrap Up

    So, folks, that is all that there is to know about blockchain development and how to become a blockchain developer. Now go ahead and master the skills mentioned above to get into blockchain development.

    If you have read this far I really appreciate it.

    Let's get social 😊 Twitter | GitHub | YouTube | LinkedIn |

    Enjoy Coding ❤

    Jagad Yudha Awali - Apr 20

item.title: 使用 GitHub 在 Heroku 上部署您的 Node.js 应用程序 | Deploy your Node.js App on Heroku using GitHub
item.description:
在本教程中，我们将介绍在 Heroku 上免费部署 Node.js 应用程序的过程。 部署应用程序可能是一项乏味的任务，但如果我们正确地遵循这些步骤，那么它就是小菜一碟。

当我部署我的第一个应用程序时，我必须浏览许多文章才能了解完整的步骤，为了节省您的时间，我在这里列出了部署应用程序的分步过程。

要开始使用，您需要一个可以轻松创建的 heroku 帐户

在这里，我采用了一个简单的演示项目来演示部署。

这就是我们的 Node.js 项目的样子，它只包含一个 index.html 文件，该文件将呈现在网站的主页上。

要初始化 git 存储库，请在项目目录中打开一个终端并运行以下命令。

或者如果您的项目已经有一个 git repo，那么您可以跳过这部分 :)

环境变量允许我们配置我们的应用程序而无需重新构建它。 它将应用程序的特定环境方面外部化。

环境变量有助于配置参数，例如

  1. 运行应用程序的端口。

  2. 如果您的项目使用 API 或任何其他包含敏感信息的东西。

首先，您需要安装可以在终端中完成的“dotenv”包

你需要添加到你的 index.js 文件

require('dotenv).config();

现在，在您的项目目录中创建一个 .env 文件并添加您要在外部配置的参数。

同样，您可以添加其他参数。

要在您的应用程序中使用这些变量，您可以使用

在您的索引文件中。

在上面的示例中，我使用上面所述的语法添加了端口。

重要的！
不要忘记将您的 .env 文件添加到 .gitignore，因为如果您在将项目推送到 GitHub 时不这样做，您的 API 密钥和敏感数据将被暴露。 还将 node_modules 文件夹添加到 .gitignore。

完成上述步骤后，您的 .gitignore 文件中应该至少包含 .env 和 node_modules。

在您的项目目录中创建一个名为 Procfile 的文件，并将以下命令添加到其中。

在对项目进行更改之后，现在是提交更改的好时机，将你的 repo 推送到 GitHub，你可以按照这篇文章进行操作。

从上面的链接下载并安装 CLI。

通过运行命令登录到 CLI，它将带您进入 Heroku 登录页面，您可以在那里填写您的凭据。



  1. 在 Heroku 上创建一个新应用

创建一个新应用程序，它会提示您输入应用程序的名称。

如果您的应用程序使用 API 密钥并且您已将其添加到 .env 文件中，则：

  1. 转到设置

  2. 在 config vars 部分中，在键值输入中添加 API 密钥或您的密钥。

    注意：key、values 中的输入应与您在 .env 文件中给出的变量名称匹配

嗯，这是相当多的工作;）

现在在部署部分的应用程序仪表板中，选择部署方法为 GitHub。

连接存储库后，只需向下滚动并部署您的应用程序，经过一两分钟的处理，它会要求您访问已部署的应用程序。

在我们的例子中，这就是它的外观 xD。

恭喜您已成功部署 Node.js 应用程序，现在您可以在世界面前展示您的项目了 :)

如果您喜欢本教程，请随时在下面发表评论。 接受反馈。

您可以在Twitter 、 GitHub 、 LinkedIn上与我联系，我很乐意回答您的任何问题！



原文:

In this tutorial, we will go over the process of deploying a Node.js application on Heroku for free. Deploying an application can be a tedious task but if we follow the steps properly then it's a cakewalk.

When I was deploying my first app I had to browse many articles in order to know the complete steps, to save your time here I've listed step-by-step procedures to deploy your app.

To get started you will require a heroku account that you can easily create

Here I’ve taken a simple demo project to demonstrate the deployment.

This is how our Node.js project looks like, it only contains an index.html file that will be rendered on the homepage of the website.

1. Initialize a git repository in the project

To initialize a git repository, open a terminal in your project directory and run the following command.

or if your project already has a git repo then you can skip this part :)

2. Configure your app using Environment Variables

Environment variables allow us to configure our application without re-building it. It externalizes the environment-specific aspects of the application.

Environment variables are helpful to configure parameters such as

  1. The port on which your application is running.

  2. If your project uses an API or any other thing that contains sensitive information.

First, you need to install the ‘dotenv’ package which can be done in your terminal

you need to add to your index.js file

require('dotenv).config();

Now, create a .env file in your project directory and add the parameters that you want to configure externally.

similarly, you can add other parameters.

To use these variables in your app you can access them using

“process.env.” in your index file.

In the above example, I've added the port using the syntax I've stated above.

IMPORTANT!
Do not forget to add your .env file to .gitignore because if you don't do that when you push your project to GitHub your API keys and sensitive data will be exposed. Also add node_modules folder to .gitignore.

After following the above steps your .gitignore file should have at least .env and node_modules in it.

3. Create a Procfile

A Procfile is a file that specifies the commands that are executed by the app on startup.

Create a file named Procfile into your project directory and add the below command to it.

after making the changes to the project it is a good time to commit your changes, to push your repo to GitHub you can follow this article.

4. Download Heroku CLI and Sign In

The Heroku CLI

Download and install the CLI from the above link.

Log in into the CLI by running the command and it will take you to Heroku login page and you can fill your credentials there.
  1. Create a new app on Heroku

Create a new app and it will prompt you to enter the name of the app.

If your application uses an API key and you have added it in .env file then:

  1. Go to the settings

  2. In config vars section add the API keys or your secret keys in the key-value input.

    Note: the inputs in key, values should match with the names of the variables you have given in .env file

6. Connecting your GitHub Repository with Heroku and deploying your application

Well, that was quite a lot of work ;)

Now in your application dashboard in the deploy section, select the deployment method as GitHub.

After your repository is connected just scroll down and deploy your app and after a minute or two of processing it will ask you to visit your application that has been deployed.

In our case this is how it looks xD.

Congratulations you have successfully deployed your Node.js application, now you can flex your projects in front of the world :)

If you liked this tutorial, please feel free to leave a comment below. Open to feedback.

You can connect with me on Twitter, GitHub, LinkedIn, I would love to answer any question that you have!
item.author: kunal4now

item.title: 无服务器缓存 | Serverless cache
item.description:
我发明了无服务器缓存服务吗？ 我稍后会回答这个问题。

我最近对无服务器多区域配置感兴趣，结果发现有许多 AWS 服务可以帮助您实现多区域，例如：

  • 我确信我错过了更多，但是在构建原型应用程序时，我开始考虑如何缓存数据。 最著名的内存服务是：

    • 亚马逊 ElastiCache

    如果需要在微秒内生成响应，则必须使用 DAX 或类似的内存缓存。 例如，DAX 提高了重读应用程序的吞吐量并降低了操作复杂性，因为它的使用对于 DynamoDB 是透明的。

    无论内存缓存的用例如何，我在帖子中提到Amazon DynamoDB 如何适应多区域场景，不可能使用 DAX，因为它会在同一区域内复制自身。 因此，要实现多区域，我必须在每个区域部署一个 DAX 集群，并创建一些复制和故障转移机制。

    我确信我将描述的场景对每个人来说都不一样，但请耐心等待。

    想象一下拥有不同帐户的两个团队。 每个组可以或不可以实现他们的缓存逻辑并在 CloudFront 中缓存一些东西，在一些内存服务中缓存一些东西。

    我问自己是否可以绕过并简化跨团队的架构，我想出了将 DynamoDB 全局表与 CloudFront 结合使用的想法。

    很明显，内存服务和 CloudFront 之间不能进行一对一的比较。 但是，基于需求和权衡，我认为它可能是一个不错的选择，主要是因为涉及的操作任务较少。

    使用 CloudFront 和 DynamoDB 进行缓存

    缓存，简单来说，就是用来加速应用程序，避免对多次请求同一事物的应用程序服务造成压力。

    我的目标是找到一种替代方法来集中跨团队的缓存并消除对内存集群的需求。 让我们看几个例子：

    • 此无服务器缓存的结果将具有以下流程：

      如果前端团队多次向后端团队请求，例如ID=2的对象，该团队有两种选择：

      • 每次查询其数据源
      • 查询一次并放入缓存中，并从缓存中返回对象

      一个好的做法是在特定时间缓存来自数据源的响应并返回此版本，而不会使数据源不堪重负并保持准备就绪，也许可以用于其他操作，即使这意味着设置非常低的 TTL。

      前端团队和后端团队之间总是存在网络延迟，因此前端团队也可以决定缓存来自他身边的后端团队已经缓存的响应。 通常，这样做有两个原因：

      • 帮助后端团队扩大规模，避免不必要的请求
      • 避免调用后端团队的额外延迟，并更好地响应最终用户。

      前端团队可能决定将来自后端的所有响应缓存到内存服务中并应用 TryGetValue 模式。

      前端团队还可以决定：

      • 他们不需要微毫秒访问数据
      • 他们不想维护内存中的集群

      因此，他们的权衡是使用 CloudFront 并将所有内容存储在离用户更近的地方，并接受这样的想法，即如果他们需要使缓存键无效或覆盖，这不像内存中的缓存那样直接。 最终的一致性权衡。

      此外，由于它的按需容量，我无需担心请求量的不确定性，而使用内存中的集群，我需要提供正确的大小并在需要时向上和向下扩展。 借助全局表，DynamoDB 将写入传播到不同区域的其他副本表。 在多区域设计中，您可以更靠近最终用户进行查询。

      在一个典型的应用场景中，如果用户产生一个请求并且它不存在于缓存中，那么该请求将到达将被检索到的源。 这是最终用户体验的最坏情况。 但是，有预热缓存的概念。 本质上，它将在最终用户请求之前将项目放入缓存中，从而获得更好的体验，因为现在信息是从缓存中提供的。

      此项目中的缓存是 CloudFront，预热流程将需要 DynamoDB 作为最终用户和源之间的中间人。

      作为一个团队，我可以通过多种方式预热缓存：

      • 构建一个爬虫，它将遍历我的网站页面并在项目不在缓存中时点击原点
      • 每个团队都实现他们的逻辑来调用 CloudFront 以生成丢失的密钥并调用源
      • 创建无服务器缓存服务

      无服务器缓存服务可用于单区域和多区域设置。 然而，对于多区域，与缓存的斗争更加明显，因为目前，提供的内存服务在单个区域中复制它们自己。

      多区域基础架构将需要以下内容：

      • 缓存服务由具有两条路由的端点组成：

        • 如果端点之一出现故障，Route53 会将请求重定向到以下最佳延迟端点。

          如何使用 DynamoDB？

          我现在可以发出事件、写入 DynamoDB、设置 TTL 并异步查找 CloudFront，而不是轰炸源，因为 TryGetValue 模式将直接缓存到 CloudFront。

          理论上，使用 TryGetValue 模式，我可以删除所有 DynamoDB 部分。 但是，想象一下我想预热一万或十万个项目。 我可以异步执行此操作，分散查询而不冒消耗应用程序的所有资源容量的风险。 相反，使用 TryGetValue 流，我将对整个系统运行所有请求。

          有了异步选项，我还可以通过监听事件来预热缓存，从而获得更大的灵活性。

使用内存集群

内存架构需要：

  • 每个区域中的一个集群
  • 修补和管理集群
  • 跨区域移动数据的同步系统

可选用于处理：

  • 应用程序负载均衡器而不是调用 AWS Lambda 函数的 APIGW。

例如，在这个模式中，我认为前端团队必须为他们的应用程序缓存数据，并且当出现未命中或其他问题时，会将数据请求给后端团队，后端团队也会在内部缓存他们的数据。

无服务器方式

借助 CloudFront 和 DynamoDB，我简化了维护内存集群的操作任务，集中了缓存，并找到了一种可接受的替代方法来使用缓存跨团队和更接近最终用户。

考虑到应用程序无论如何都会调用前端团队中的端点，现在，使用跨团队共享的缓存密钥策略，响应时间将只是到达 CloudFront 的网络延迟，这与调用后端团队。 当然，这并没有涵盖世界上所有的情况，但是我几乎腾出了后端团队的资源，减少了对他们的大部分请求，这只会浪费资源来加载数据缓存。 由于不必要的流量减少，我现在拥有资源/配额来服务不需要缓存的请求。

关于 PoC 的一些话

这个 PoC 的所有代码都可以在我的GitHub 存储库中找到，您可以在其中查看部署基础架构的概念和模板。 因为它是一个 PoC，所以我冒昧地省略了以下内容：

  • 我假设使用了 CloudFront 的自定义域

没有它们部署有点麻烦，所以需要一些手动配置，但模板就在那里。

您可以在 Serverlessland的 Amazon API Gateway 前面找到 Amazon Route 53 延迟策略路由的模板。 后者很容易编辑，为当前模板添加了一些更改：

 ########################################################################## # Parameters # ########################################################################## Parameters: ApiPrimaryEndpoint: Type: String ApiSecondaryEndpoint: Type: String AlternateDomain: Type: String ... omitted for readability ########################################################################## # CloudFront::Distribution # ########################################################################## CloudfrontDistribution: Type: AWS::CloudFront::Distribution Properties: DistributionConfig: .... Aliases: - !Ref AlternateDomain ViewerCertificate: AcmCertificateArn: <your ACM Certificate ARN> MinimumProtocolVersion: TLSv1.2_2021 SslSupportMethod: sni-only ....

如果有未命中，则直接从源请求它并将其缓存在 CloudFront 中，作为未命中，如果 Dynamodb 项目中的数据已过期但尚未从 DynamoDB 生存时间 (TTL) 中删除，也会被视为未命中。

  • 为了模拟 TryGetValue 模式，URL 是：

     //the origin_url can also be in the header, but for PoC, I put it in the query string. https://<ideally alias cloud front domain>/cache?key=daniele&origin_url=https://<origin domain>/origin?key=daniele

    预热缓存，这是一个 POST

     //https://<route53 custom domain or API endpoint>/set-cache { "key": "daniele", "data": "whatever I want, string, number, json", "cdnTTL": 120, "clientTTL": 10 }

    它将内容添加到 DynamoDB 中，将 TTL 设置为与 CDN (cdnTTL) 具有相同值的记录。 我可以使用 Cache-Control 标头控制 CDN 和浏览器中的缓存。

     Cache-Control: "public, max-age=10, s-maxage=120;".

    设置缓存的人可能负责生存时间。
    如果您想了解更多信息，请阅读此处。

    测试结果

    源 Lambda 中的代码添加了一个从 20ms 到 1s 的随机延迟来模拟一些调用。 所以这部分并没有增加多少价值。 尽管如此，我使用随机密钥运行 1K RPS，导致 1K RPS 未命中和对源的 1K RPS 请求。

    现在我在 CloudFront 中有或多或少的 600K 热键，我可以模拟应用程序从边缘检索它们的步骤。

    我看到的是，现在后端没有按预期接到电话，响应时间只是网络延迟：

    这个测试是我的观点之一。 使用 CloudFront，我们现在已将 60 万个请求保存到后端，这很可能以相同或多或少的延迟为来自其内存缓存的所有请求提供服务。

    不是所有闪闪发光的都是金子

    正如我在开头提到的，这种设计并不适用于所有用例，本节将重点介绍一些。

    应用程序的一致性模型是什么？ 我可以提供过时的数据吗？ 对于许多用例来说，这改变了游戏规则，因此 CloudFront 的这个想法和他的缓慢驱逐不适用。 但也许只有 DynamoDB 可以作为一种选择，并以其他方式管理记录的 TTL（扫描、删除或其他应用程序逻辑）

    这可能是缓存预热期间的限制。 而且，如果要异步做，像SQS、SNS这样的服务有256KB的限制，这也是要考虑的。 我可以使用 S3 或将 APIGW 同步到 Lambda，但 400KB 的限制仍然适用。

    如果我可以权衡使用 DynamoDB，也许我可以考虑另外两个选择：

    • 从架构中完全移除 DynamoDB 并仅使用 TryGetValue 模式
    • 使用S3 多区域访问点将DynamoDB 替换为 S3 作为预热用例

    在这个 PoC 中，我没有保护对 CloudFront 中内容的访问。 考虑这部分也很重要，因此，我添加了一些额外的读数：

    结论

    我发明了无服务器缓存服务吗？ 你怎么看？

    我确信我不是第一个在这方面有创造力的人，但我认为这是一个需要权衡取舍的替代方案。

    我通过这种设计将成本转移到 CloudFront，而不是一些内存中的集群。 因此，值得一提的是，价格因地理区域而异，并且基于您的内容分发的边缘位置，但默认情况下。

    通过从边缘位置交付内容，我使用 CloudFront 最大限度地减少了最终用户延迟并最大限度地提高了带宽速度。

    使用无服务器缓存，我不需要修补、维护扩展/缩减、跨区域复制内存中集群的数据，仅此一项就可以节省大量资金。 请记住，内存中的集群不会扩展到零，因此即使没有使用，您也总会有一些机器在运行。

    使用 max-age 指令应该可以最大限度地减少 CloudFront 将数据传输到可以缓存在用户浏览器中的内容的成本，因为它不必从边缘站点的缓存中获取它们。 不过，另一方面，这种设计在 CloudFront 端缺少快速/覆盖密钥失效。

    一切都是取舍。 这是你的选择。



    • 原文:

    Did I invent a serverless cache service? I will answer this question later on.

    I recently got interested in serverless multi-region configuration, and it turned out there are many AWS services that will help you to achieve a multi-region like:

    • CloudFront failover configuration
    • Route53 routing policies
    • DynamoDB global tables

    I am sure I miss many more, but while building a prototype application, I started to consider how to cache data. The most famous in-memory services are:

    • Amazon DynamoDB Accelerator (DAX)
    • Amazon ElastiCache

    DAX or a similar in-memory cache must be used if there is the need to generate a response in microseconds. DAX, for example, increase throughput for heavy read applications and reduces operational complexity because its usage is transparent with DynamoDB.

    Regardless of the use cases for in-memory cache, I mentioned in the post how Amazon DynamoDB fit in a multi-region scenario, it is not possible to use DAX because it replicates itself within the same region. Therefore, to achieve multi-region, I must deploy a DAX cluster in each region and create some replication and failover mechanism.

    I am sure the scenario I will describe is not the same for everybody but bear with me.

    Imagine two teams with different accounts. Each group could or could not implement their caching logic and cache something in CloudFront and something in some in-memory service.

    I asked myself if I could go around and simplify the architecture cross-team, and I came up with the idea to use DynamoDB global tables in conjunction with CloudFront.

    It is already evident that it cannot be a one to one comparison between an in-memory service and CloudFront. However, based on the requirements and trade-off, I think it could be a good alternative, mainly because fewer operational tasks are involved.

    Caching with CloudFront and DynamoDB

    Cache, in very simple words, is used to speed up the application and avoid to stress the application services requesting multiple times the same thing.

    My goal is to find an alternative way to centralise cache across teams and remove the need for in-memory clusters. Let's see a few examples:

    • CloudFront as alternative
    • DynamoDB as alternative

    The results of this serverless cache will have the following flow:

    CloudFront as alternative

    If the front-end team request multiple times, for example, an object with ID=2, to a back-end team, this team has two options:

    • each time, make a query to its data source
    • query once and put it in the cache, and return the object from the cache

    A good practice is to cache for a specific time the response from the data source and return this version without overwhelming the data source and keep it ready, maybe for other operations, even if this means setting a very low TTL.

    There is always a network latency between the front-end team and back-end team, so the front-end team could also decide to cache the already cached response from the back-end team on his side. Usually, this is made for two reasons:

    • help the back-end team to scale more avoid unnecessary requests
    • avoid the extra latency to call the back-end team and be more responsive to the end-user.

    The front-end team maybe decide to cache all the responses from the back-end into an in-memory service and apply the TryGetValue pattern.

    A TryGetValue pattern will try to retrieve the object from the cache, and if there is a miss will go to the origin and save it into the cache.

    The front-end team can also decide:

    • they do not need micro milliseconds access to data
    • they do not want to maintain an in-memory cluster

    So their trade-off is to use CloudFront and store everything there closer to the user and live with the idea that if they need to invalidate or overwrite a cache key, this is not immediate like the in-memory cache. Eventual consistency trade-off.

    DynamoDB as alternative

    DynamoDB is fast, and the typical latency is in the order of 10ms response. Moreover, thanks to its on-demand capacity, I don't need to worry about the uncertain volume of requests, while with an in-memory cluster, I need to provision the correct size and scale up and down if needed. Thanks to global tables, DynamoDB propagates the write to the other replica tables in the different regions. In a multi-region design, you can query closer to the end-user.

    In a typical application scenario, if a user generates a request and it is not present in the cache, the request will reach the origin where it will be retrieved. This is the worst-case scenario for an end-user experience. However, there is the concept of warming up the cache. Essentially it will put the items in the cache before end-user requests resulting in a better experience because now the information is served from the cache.

    The cache in this project is CloudFront, and the warming up flow will require DynamoDB as the middle man between the end-user and the origin.

    As a team, I can warm up the cache in multiple ways:

    • build a crawler that will go through my website pages and hit the origin when items are not in the cache
    • each team implement their logic to call CloudFront to generate a missing key and call the origin
    • create a serverless cache service

    The serverless cache service can be used in a single and multi-region setup. However, with multi-region, the struggle with the cache is more evident because currently, the in-memory services provided replicate themself in the single region.

    A multi-region infrastructure will require the following:

    • CloudFront failover configuration
    • Route53 Latency routing

    The cache service is made of an endpoint with two routes:

    • GET - TryGetValue pattern
    • POST - warming up the cache

    CloudFront is used in front of the cache service. CloudFront origins will point to the APIGW endpoint, and because it is multi-region, each origin will be in a different AWS region.

    The POST route is used to warm up the cache, and this will require:

    • APIGW custom domain
    • Route53 latency routing pointing to the APIGW custom domains.

    Route53 will redirect the request to the following best latency endpoint if one of the endpoints goes down.

    How is DynamoDB used?

    DynamoDB is used only for the warming cache scenario without stressing the origin with many requests. Instead of bombarding the origin, I can now emit events, write into DynamoDB, set a TTL and lookup CloudFront asynchronously because the TryGetValue pattern will cache directly into CloudFront.

    In theory, with the TryGetValue pattern, I could remove all the DynamoDB parts. However, imagine I want to warm up ten thousand or 100 thousand items. I can do this asynchronously, spreading the queries without risking consuming all the resource capacity of the application. Using the TryGetValue flow instead, I will run all the requests against the entire system.

    Having the async option, I can also warm up the cache by listening to events gaining more flexibility.

    Using in-memory cluster

    An in-memory architecture requires:

    • A cluster in each region
    • Patching and managing the clusters
    • Synchronisation system to move data across the region

    Optionally for processing:

    • APIGW + Lambda inside the VPC with relative limitations
    • ECS Cluster and another cluster to deploy and maintain.
    • Application Load Balancer instead of APIGW that invokes an AWS Lambda function.

    In this schema, for example, I consider a front-end team that must cache data for their apps and, when there is a miss or something else, will request the data to a back-end team that will cache internally also their data.

    Serverless way

    With CloudFront and DynamoDB, I have simplified the operational tasks for maintaining an in-memory cluster, centralised the cache and found an acceptable alternative way to use a cache cross-team and closer to end-users.

    Considering that the applications will call the endpoint in the front-end team anyway, now, with a cache key policy shared cross-team, the response time will be just network latency to reach the CloudFront, which is pretty much the same as calling the back-end team. Of course, this is not covering all the cases in the world, but I pretty much free up the resources of the back-end team, cutting the majority of the request to them, which would be just a waste of resources to load data from the cache. With less unnecessary traffic, I have now resources/quotas to serve requests that do not require cache.

    Some words about the PoC

    All the code of this PoC is available on my GitHub repository, where you can see the concepts and templates to deploy the infrastructure. Because it is a PoC, I took the liberty to omit something like:

    • Amazon Route 53 Latency policy route front of Amazon API Gateway
    • I assume a custom domain with CloudFront is used

    The deployment is a bit cumbersome without them, so some manual configuration is needed, but the templates are there.

    You can find the template for Amazon Route 53 Latency policy route in front of Amazon API Gateway in Serverlessland. The latter is easily editable, adding some changes to the current template:

    ##########################################################################
#  Parameters                                                            #
##########################################################################
Parameters:
  ApiPrimaryEndpoint:
    Type: String
  ApiSecondaryEndpoint:
    Type: String
  AlternateDomain:
    Type: String

... omitted for readability

##########################################################################
#  CloudFront::Distribution                                              #
##########################################################################
  CloudfrontDistribution:
    Type: AWS::CloudFront::Distribution
    Properties:
      DistributionConfig:
....
        Aliases:
          - !Ref AlternateDomain
        ViewerCertificate:
          AcmCertificateArn: <your ACM Certificate ARN>
          MinimumProtocolVersion: TLSv1.2_2021
          SslSupportMethod: sni-only
....

    The PoC contains:

    • A CloudFront function - fixing the browsers cache behaviour
    • Lambda function: set-cache - warming up the cache, adding into DynamoDB with a TTL for expiration, and making the lookup in CloudFront with the same TLL to keep them in sync.
    • Lambda function: get-cache - retrieve the data from DynamoDB. If there is a miss, request it directly from the origin and cache it in CloudFront, and as a miss, it is also considered if data in the Dynamodb item is expired but not yet deleted from the DynamoDB Time to Live (TTL).
    • Lambda function: origin - simulate the origin back-end team where I simulate a call to a source with a Thread.Sleep and return a JSON

    To simulate the TryGetValue pattern, the URL is:

    //the origin_url can also be in the header, but for PoC, I put it in the query string.
https://<ideally alias cloud front domain>/cache?key=daniele&origin_url=https://<origin domain>/origin?key=daniele

    Warming up the cache, it is a POST to

    //https://<route53 custom domain or API endpoint>/set-cache
{
    "key": "daniele",
    "data": "whatever I want, string, number, json",
    "cdnTTL": 120,
    "clientTTL": 10
}

    It will add the content into DynamoDB, setting the TTL to the record with the same value as the CDN (cdnTTL). I can control the cache in the CDN and Browser with the Cache-Control headers.

     Cache-Control: "public, max-age=10, s-maxage=120;".

    Who is setting the cache could be in charge of the time to live.
    The "max-age" directive tells the browser how long the response should be cached in seconds, while the "s-maxage" directive is for CloudFront. If you want to know more, please read here.

    Test results

    The code in the origin Lambda adds a random delay from 20ms to 1s to simulate some calls. So this part does not add much value. Nevertheless, I run 1K RPS with random keys resulting in 1K RPS miss and 1K RPS requests to the origin.

    Now I have more or less 600K warmed keys in CloudFront, and I can simulate the steps where the application will retrieve them from the edge.

    What I can see is that now the back-end did not receive a call as expected, and the response time is just network latency:

    This test is one of my points. Using CloudFront, we have now saved 600K requests to the back-end, which was most likely serving all the requests from their in-memory cache for the same more or less latency.

    Not all that glitters is gold

    As I mentioned at the start, this design will not apply to all use cases, and this section will highlight a few.

    What is the consistency model of the application? Can I serve stale data? This is a game-changer for many use cases, so this idea with CloudFront and his slow evictions cannot apply. But maybe only DynamoDB could be an option and manage the TTL of the records in other ways (Scan, Delete or other application logic)

    ElasticCache offers more data structure than DynamoDB and larger objects. DynamoDB has a max size of 400KB. This could be a limitation during the warming up of the cache. Moreover, if you want to do it asynchronously, services like SQS and SNS have a limit of 256KB, which is also to consider. I can go around with S3 or synchronously APIGW to Lambda, but the 400KB limit still applies.

    If I can have trade-offs on using DynamoDB, maybe I can consider two other options:

    • Removing completely DynamoDB from the architecture and using only the TryGetValue pattern
    • Replacing DynamoDB with S3 as warming up use case, using the S3 Multi-Region Access Points

    In this PoC, I did not protect the access of the content in CloudFront. CloudFront provides several options for securing content that it delivers. It is important also to consider this part, and because of this, I add some extra readings:

    Conclusion

    Did I invent a serverless cache service? What do you think?

    I am sure I am not the first to get inventive on this, but I think it is an alternative with some trade-offs to consider.

    I shift the cost to the CloudFront with this design instead of some in-memory cluster. So it is worth saying the prices vary across geographic regions and are based on the edge location where your content is distributed but by default.

    I have minimised end-user latency and maximised bandwidth speed with CloudFront by delivering content from edge locations.

    With serverless cache, I do not need to patch, maintain scale up/down, replicate data of my in-memory cluster across regions, and that alone is a considerable saving. Remember, the in-memory cluster does not scale to zero, so you have always some machine running even without usage.

    Using the max-age directive should minimise CloudFront costs for data transfer out on things that can be cached inside the user browser because it doesn't have to fetch them from the cache in the edge location. Still, on the other hand, this design lacks a quick/overwrite key invalidation on the CloudFront side.

    Everything is a trade-off. The choice is yours.
